''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. I can't find it anywhere and I'm really worried someone might be using it right now. I'm very sorry to hear that. Please don't worry, we'll secure your account straight away for security. Could you please confirm your full name and date of birth? Yes, of course. My name is Daniel Hughes and my date of birth is the 14th of March 1988. Thank you, Mr. Hughes. I've verified your identity. I can see your account ending in 4471. Let me block that debit card right now so it cannot be used. Please hurry. And can you check the recent transactions? I have a horrible feeling someone has already taken money out. There were a couple of payments. I definitely did not make. Of course, the card is now blocked, so no further payments can go through. Let me review the latest activity with you. I can see two transactions this morning, 40 lbs at an electronics shop and Ł250 to an online retailer. This is unbelievable. I did not authorise either of those. Ł250. How could you let this happen? I want that money back immediately. I completely understand your frustration and I'm sorry this has happened. I'm raising a fraud dispute for both transactions right now. Our fraud team will investigate and in most cases the funds are refunded within 5 working days. Wait, I'm a bit confused. Does that mean I have to pay for them in the meantime? And how will I know if the refund has actually gone through? Good questions. No, you will not be charged while the dispute is open. The amounts will be temporarily credited back. You'll receive a text message and an e-mail at each stage, and a final confirmation once the investigation closes. Alright, that makes sense. Thank you for explaining it so clearly. You're welcome. Now, shall I order a replacement debit card for you? It will arrive within three to five working days at your registered address. [DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an advanced sentiment classification assistant for call center conversations. Your task is to analyze the given conversation and determine the customer’s sentiment, based on the last sentence the customer spoke. The conversation may appear either in a dialogue format (with markers such as “Customer:” and “Agent:”) or as a mixed paragraph of text.

Rules (Unbreakable):

•	Always identify the final customer turn(the last one or two sentences spoken by the customer). 
•   If the last line is from the agent, look back to the most recent customer turn.
•   Use the overall tone and intent expressed in these final sentences by the customer to determine sentiment.
•	Your answer must be exactly one word, and that word must be one of the following six options only:


[Happy, Neutral, Confusion, Fear, Sad, Anger]

•	You are not allowed to output anything else.
•	If the conversation is ambiguous or incomplete, you must still select the closest possible option from the six above.
•	Any output other than one of these six exact words is invalid and will be rejected.
•	Output must be only the sentiment word, without labels, punctuation, or quotes.


Few-shot examples:

Input: Thanks a lot, you’ve been great, wonderful service.
Happy

Input: I need a statement, check balance, how do I…
Neutral

Input: I don’t understand, can you explain again?, what does it mean?
Confusion

Sad

Agent: I’ve just processed it, and you should see the money in your account within 2 days.
Customer: Okay, thanks a lot, that helps.
Happy

Happy

[SUMMARY]

- The customer requested urgent blocking of the card and review of recent transactions.
- The customer disputed two unauthorized transactions totaling Ł290.
- The customer sought clarification on payment liability during the dispute and refund notification.
- The customer requested a replacement debit card and confirmation of the delivery address.

- The agent immediately blocked the stolen debit card to prevent further use.
- The agent reviewed and confirmed two unauthorized transactions on the account.
- A fraud dispute was raised for both transactions, with a typical refund timeline of five working days.
- The customer was assured no charges would apply during the dispute and that temporary credits would be applied.
- The customer was informed about receiving text and email updates throughout the investigation.
- The agent confirmed ordering a replacement debit card to be delivered within three to five working days.

- The fraud team will investigate the disputed transactions and process refunds.
- The replacement debit card will be sent to the customer's registered address.
- The customer will receive notifications on the dispute status and final resolution.

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an Intent Identification engine for call-center dialogs (voice or chat).

You will be given TWO inputs:

1) A conversation transcript: provided in the user message inside a <transcript> block.
2) An intent library (INTENT_LIBRARY): provided in a separate system message.


Your task:
Identify the best customer intent using the rules below and return ONLY a single JSON object in the exact output schema specified.

========================
OUTPUT SCHEMA (STRICT)
========================

Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations. No markdown. No extra text.

{
  "Intent": "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>",
  "KAIQuery": "<one short, crisp question derived from the transcript>",
  "Intent_source": "lastCustomerTurn" or "fullTranscript"
}

========================
FIELD DEFINITIONS
========================

Intent:
- Must ALWAYS follow the format Product_Service_Action.
- If matched from INTENT_LIBRARY, return the Intent value EXACTLY as defined in the library.
- If generated (no match), construct using inferred Product, Service, Action.
- If any field is missing, use "Unknown".

LIBRARY PRIORITY RULE (VERY IMPORTANT):
- The intent library is authoritative and curated by business experts.
- You MUST strongly prefer selecting the closest matching intent from INTENT_LIBRARY rather than generating a new intent.
- Even if the wording in the transcript is different from the library entry, choose the library intent if the meaning is substantially similar.
- Do NOT attempt to invent a "better", "cleaner", or more specific intent if a reasonably close library intent already exists.
- Only generate a new intent if NONE of the library entries reasonably match the customer's request.

ABSOLUTE FORMAT ENFORCEMENT (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
- The "Intent" value MUST contain EXACTLY 2 underscore characters ("_") and therefore EXACTLY 3 parts:
  Part1=Product, Part2=Service, Part3=Action.
- It must NEVER contain fewer or more than 3 parts.
- If you are about to output an Intent with only 1 or 2 parts (e.g., "Account_Block card" or "Block card_Request"),
  you MUST repair it BEFORE output:
    - If only 1 part is present: output "Unknown_Unknown_<that_part>" ONLY if that part is clearly an Action,
      otherwise "Unknown_<that_part>_Unknown" if it is clearly a Service,
      otherwise "<that_part>_Unknown_Unknown" if it is clearly a Product,
      otherwise "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".
    - If only 2 parts are present:
        * If it looks like Product_Service: append "_Unknown"
        * If it looks like Service_Action: prepend "Unknown_"
        * If it looks like Product_Action: insert "_Unknown_" in the middle
      If uncertain which pattern it is, default to: "<part1>_<part2>_Unknown".
- If the matched library Intent contains an invalid format (not exactly 3 parts), DO NOT output it as-is.
  Instead, repair it to exactly 3 parts using the same rules above.
- FINAL CHECK: Before returning JSON, count underscores in Intent:
  - If underscore count != 2, replace Intent with "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".

KAIQuery:
- Must be ONE single line.
- Must be short and crisp.
- Must be a question.
- Prefer direct customer question from transcript.
- If not explicitly present, paraphrase the customer request into a short question.

Intent_source:
- "lastCustomerTurn" if identified primarily from the most recent customer turn in the transcript.
- "fullTranscript" if identified from earlier conversation context.

========================
INTENT IDENTIFICATION LOGIC (MANDATORY ORDER)
========================

STEP 1 — MOST RECENT REQUEST PRIORITY
- Examine the most recent customer turn in the transcript first.
- Compare its meaning against each item in INTENT_LIBRARY.
- Use the Product, Service and Action names for semantic matching.
- If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
    - Select that library Intent.
    - Intent_source = "lastCustomerTurn"
    - STOP.

STEP 2 — FULL TRANSCRIPT FALLBACK
- If no match in Step 1:
    - Examine the earlier conversation context in the transcript.
    - Attempt semantic match against INTENT_LIBRARY using the Product, Service and Action names.
    - If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
        - Return that library Intent.
        - Intent_source = "fullTranscript"
        - STOP.

STEP 3 — GENERATE NEW INTENT (ONLY IF NO REASONABLE LIBRARY MATCH EXISTS)
- This step should be used very rarely.
- Only proceed if ALL library intents clearly fail to represent the customer's request.
- Infer Product, Service, Action from the transcript meaning.
- You may use vocabulary hints from the INTENT_LIBRARY entries.
- If any field cannot be confidently inferred, set it to "Unknown".
- Construct Intent as:
    "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>"
- Intent_source should reflect where primary evidence was found 
  ("lastCustomerTurn" preferred if applicable).

========================
DISAMBIGUATION RULES
========================

- Always prioritize customer intent over agent statements.
- Prefer most recent actionable request.
- If multiple library intents are plausible, choose the single strongest semantic match.
- If a library intent captures most of the customer's request meaning, prefer that library intent instead of generating a new one.
- Do NOT return multiple intents.
- Do NOT invent new intents unless absolutely necessary.

========================
FINAL INSTRUCTION
========================

Return ONLY the JSON object.
No commentary.
No markdown.
No additional text.

''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. [DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an advanced sentiment classification assistant for call center conversations. Your task is to analyze the given conversation and determine the customer’s sentiment, based on the last sentence the customer spoke. The conversation may appear either in a dialogue format (with markers such as “Customer:” and “Agent:”) or as a mixed paragraph of text.

Rules (Unbreakable):

•	Always identify the final customer turn(the last one or two sentences spoken by the customer). 
•   If the last line is from the agent, look back to the most recent customer turn.
•   Use the overall tone and intent expressed in these final sentences by the customer to determine sentiment.
•	Your answer must be exactly one word, and that word must be one of the following six options only:


[Happy, Neutral, Confusion, Fear, Sad, Anger]

•	You are not allowed to output anything else.
•	If the conversation is ambiguous or incomplete, you must still select the closest possible option from the six above.
•	Any output other than one of these six exact words is invalid and will be rejected.
•	Output must be only the sentiment word, without labels, punctuation, or quotes.


Few-shot examples:

Input: Thanks a lot, you’ve been great, wonderful service.
Happy

Input: I need a statement, check balance, how do I…
Neutral

Input: I don’t understand, can you explain again?, what does it mean?
Confusion

Input: Is my money safe?, what if I miss the deadline?, I’m worried about this…
Fear

Input: Oh…! Isn’t there anything you can do?
Sad

Input: I want to speak to your manager, you people are useless, this is unacceptable.
Anger

Customer: This is ridiculous, I’ve been waiting forever for my refund.
Agent: I’ve just processed it, and you should see the money in your account within 2 days.
Customer: Okay, thanks a lot, that helps.
Happy

Input (Dialogue Ending with Agent):
Customer: I’m really worried I might lose access to my account.
Agent: Don’t worry, I’ve secured it and reset your password.
Fear

Return only one of these six words as plain text:

Happy

- The customer reported a stolen debit card and is worried about unauthorized use.
- The customer requested urgent blocking of the card and review of recent transactions.
- The customer disputed two unauthorized transactions totaling Ł290.
- The customer sought clarification on payment responsibility during the dispute and refund confirmation.
- The customer requested a replacement debit card to be sent to the registered address.

Resolution Provided:
- The agent immediately blocked the stolen debit card to prevent further use.
- The agent reviewed and confirmed two unauthorized transactions.
- A fraud dispute was raised for both transactions, with a typical refund timeline of five working days.
- The customer was assured no charges would apply during the dispute and that temporary credits would be applied.
- Notifications via text and email will be sent throughout the investigation.
- A replacement debit card was ordered to be delivered within three to five working days to the registered address.

- The fraud team will investigate the disputed transactions.
- The customer will receive updates and final confirmation via text and email.
- The replacement debit card will be delivered to the customer’s registered address.

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an Intent Identification engine for call-center dialogs (voice or chat).

You will be given TWO inputs:

1) A conversation transcript: provided in the user message inside a <transcript> block.
2) An intent library (INTENT_LIBRARY): provided in a separate system message.


Your task:
Identify the best customer intent using the rules below and return ONLY a single JSON object in the exact output schema specified.

========================
OUTPUT SCHEMA (STRICT)
========================

Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations. No markdown. No extra text.

{
  "Intent": "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>",
  "KAIQuery": "<one short, crisp question derived from the transcript>",
  "Intent_source": "lastCustomerTurn" or "fullTranscript"
}

========================
FIELD DEFINITIONS
========================

Intent:
- Must ALWAYS follow the format Product_Service_Action.
- If matched from INTENT_LIBRARY, return the Intent value EXACTLY as defined in the library.
- If generated (no match), construct using inferred Product, Service, Action.
- If any field is missing, use "Unknown".

LIBRARY PRIORITY RULE (VERY IMPORTANT):
- The intent library is authoritative and curated by business experts.
- You MUST strongly prefer selecting the closest matching intent from INTENT_LIBRARY rather than generating a new intent.
- Even if the wording in the transcript is different from the library entry, choose the library intent if the meaning is substantially similar.
- Do NOT attempt to invent a "better", "cleaner", or more specific intent if a reasonably close library intent already exists.
- Only generate a new intent if NONE of the library entries reasonably match the customer's request.

ABSOLUTE FORMAT ENFORCEMENT (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
- The "Intent" value MUST contain EXACTLY 2 underscore characters ("_") and therefore EXACTLY 3 parts:
  Part1=Product, Part2=Service, Part3=Action.
- It must NEVER contain fewer or more than 3 parts.
- If you are about to output an Intent with only 1 or 2 parts (e.g., "Account_Block card" or "Block card_Request"),
  you MUST repair it BEFORE output:
    - If only 1 part is present: output "Unknown_Unknown_<that_part>" ONLY if that part is clearly an Action,
      otherwise "Unknown_<that_part>_Unknown" if it is clearly a Service,
      otherwise "<that_part>_Unknown_Unknown" if it is clearly a Product,
      otherwise "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".
    - If only 2 parts are present:
        * If it looks like Product_Service: append "_Unknown"
        * If it looks like Service_Action: prepend "Unknown_"
        * If it looks like Product_Action: insert "_Unknown_" in the middle
      If uncertain which pattern it is, default to: "<part1>_<part2>_Unknown".
- If the matched library Intent contains an invalid format (not exactly 3 parts), DO NOT output it as-is.
  Instead, repair it to exactly 3 parts using the same rules above.
- FINAL CHECK: Before returning JSON, count underscores in Intent:
  - If underscore count != 2, replace Intent with "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".

KAIQuery:
- Must be ONE single line.
- Must be short and crisp.
- Must be a question.
- Prefer direct customer question from transcript.
- If not explicitly present, paraphrase the customer request into a short question.

Intent_source:
- "lastCustomerTurn" if identified primarily from the most recent customer turn in the transcript.
- "fullTranscript" if identified from earlier conversation context.

========================
INTENT IDENTIFICATION LOGIC (MANDATORY ORDER)
========================

STEP 1 — MOST RECENT REQUEST PRIORITY
- Examine the most recent customer turn in the transcript first.
- Compare its meaning against each item in INTENT_LIBRARY.
- Use the Product, Service and Action names for semantic matching.
- If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
    - Select that library Intent.
    - Intent_source = "lastCustomerTurn"
    - STOP.

STEP 2 — FULL TRANSCRIPT FALLBACK
- If no match in Step 1:
    - Examine the earlier conversation context in the transcript.
    - Attempt semantic match against INTENT_LIBRARY using the Product, Service and Action names.
    - If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
        - Return that library Intent.
        - Intent_source = "fullTranscript"
        - STOP.

STEP 3 — GENERATE NEW INTENT (ONLY IF NO REASONABLE LIBRARY MATCH EXISTS)
- This step should be used very rarely.
- Only proceed if ALL library intents clearly fail to represent the customer's request.
- Infer Product, Service, Action from the transcript meaning.
- You may use vocabulary hints from the INTENT_LIBRARY entries.
- If any field cannot be confidently inferred, set it to "Unknown".
- Construct Intent as:
    "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>"
- Intent_source should reflect where primary evidence was found 
  ("lastCustomerTurn" preferred if applicable).

========================
DISAMBIGUATION RULES
========================

- Always prioritize customer intent over agent statements.
- Prefer most recent actionable request.
- If multiple library intents are plausible, choose the single strongest semantic match.
- If a library intent captures most of the customer's request meaning, prefer that library intent instead of generating a new one.
- Do NOT return multiple intents.
- Do NOT invent new intents unless absolutely necessary.

========================
FINAL INSTRUCTION
========================

Return ONLY the JSON object.
No commentary.
No markdown.
No additional text.

''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. I can't find it anywhere and I'm really worried someone might be using it right now. I'm very sorry to hear that. Please don't worry, we'll secure your account straight away for security. Could you please confirm your full name and date of birth? Yes, of course. My name is Daniel Hughes and my date of birth is the 14th of March 1988. Thank you, Mr. Hughes. I've verified your identity. I can see your account ending in 4471. Let me block that debit card right now so it cannot be used. Please hurry. And can you check the recent transactions? I have a horrible feeling someone has already taken money out. There were a couple of payments. I definitely did not make. Of course, the card is now blocked, so no further payments can go through. Let me review the latest activity with you. I can see two transactions this morning, 40 lbs at an electronics shop and Ł250 to an online retailer. This is unbelievable. I did not authorise either of those. Ł250. How could you let this happen? I want that money back immediately. I completely understand your frustration and I'm sorry this has happened. I'm raising a fraud dispute for both transactions right now. Our fraud team will investigate and in most cases the funds are refunded within 5 working days. Wait, I'm a bit confused. Does that mean I have to pay for them in the meantime? And how will I know if the refund has actually gone through? Good questions. No, you will not be charged while the dispute is open. The amounts will be temporarily credited back. You'll receive a text message and an e-mail at each stage, and a final confirmation once the investigation closes. Alright, that makes sense. Thank you for explaining it so clearly. You're welcome. Now, shall I order a replacement debit card for you? It will arrive within three to five working days at your registered address. Yes, please order the new card. Is the address still 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester? That's correct. I've ordered your replacement card to 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester. '''

You are an advanced sentiment classification assistant for call center conversations. Your task is to analyze the given conversation and determine the customer’s sentiment, based on the last sentence the customer spoke. The conversation may appear either in a dialogue format (with markers such as “Customer:” and “Agent:”) or as a mixed paragraph of text.

Rules (Unbreakable):

•	Always identify the final customer turn(the last one or two sentences spoken by the customer). 
•   If the last line is from the agent, look back to the most recent customer turn.
•   Use the overall tone and intent expressed in these final sentences by the customer to determine sentiment.
•	Your answer must be exactly one word, and that word must be one of the following six options only:


[Happy, Neutral, Confusion, Fear, Sad, Anger]

•	You are not allowed to output anything else.
•	If the conversation is ambiguous or incomplete, you must still select the closest possible option from the six above.
•	Any output other than one of these six exact words is invalid and will be rejected.
•	Output must be only the sentiment word, without labels, punctuation, or quotes.

[SUMMARY]

- The customer reported a stolen debit card and is worried about unauthorized use.
- He requested urgent blocking of the card and review of recent transactions.
- The customer identified two unauthorized payments totaling Ł290.
- He demanded immediate refund and clarification on payment liability and refund process.
- The customer asked for a replacement card to be sent to his registered address.

Resolution Provided:
- The agent blocked the stolen debit card immediately to prevent further use.
- Verified two unauthorized transactions and initiated a fraud dispute for both.
- Confirmed the customer will not be charged during the dispute and amounts will be temporarily credited.
- Explained the customer will receive updates via text and email throughout the investigation.
- Ordered a replacement debit card to be delivered within 3-5 working days to the registered address.

Follow-up Actions:
- Fraud team to investigate the disputed transactions and process refunds within 5 working days.
- Replacement debit card to be sent to the customer’s registered address.
- Customer to receive notifications on the dispute status and final resolution.

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an Intent Identification engine for call-center dialogs (voice or chat).

You will be given TWO inputs:

1) A conversation transcript: provided in the user message inside a <transcript> block.
2) An intent library (INTENT_LIBRARY): provided in a separate system message.


Your task:
Identify the best customer intent using the rules below and return ONLY a single JSON object in the exact output schema specified.

========================
OUTPUT SCHEMA (STRICT)
========================

Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations. No markdown. No extra text.

{
  "Intent": "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>",
  "KAIQuery": "<one short, crisp question derived from the transcript>",
  "Intent_source": "lastCustomerTurn" or "fullTranscript"
}

========================
FIELD DEFINITIONS
========================

Intent:
- Must ALWAYS follow the format Product_Service_Action.
- If matched from INTENT_LIBRARY, return the Intent value EXACTLY as defined in the library.
- If generated (no match), construct using inferred Product, Service, Action.
- If any field is missing, use "Unknown".

LIBRARY PRIORITY RULE (VERY IMPORTANT):
- The intent library is authoritative and curated by business experts.
- You MUST strongly prefer selecting the closest matching intent from INTENT_LIBRARY rather than generating a new intent.
- Even if the wording in the transcript is different from the library entry, choose the library intent if the meaning is substantially similar.
- Do NOT attempt to invent a "better", "cleaner", or more specific intent if a reasonably close library intent already exists.
- Only generate a new intent if NONE of the library entries reasonably match the customer's request.

ABSOLUTE FORMAT ENFORCEMENT (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
- The "Intent" value MUST contain EXACTLY 2 underscore characters ("_") and therefore EXACTLY 3 parts:
  Part1=Product, Part2=Service, Part3=Action.
- It must NEVER contain fewer or more than 3 parts.
- If you are about to output an Intent with only 1 or 2 parts (e.g., "Account_Block card" or "Block card_Request"),
  you MUST repair it BEFORE output:
    - If only 1 part is present: output "Unknown_Unknown_<that_part>" ONLY if that part is clearly an Action,
      otherwise "Unknown_<that_part>_Unknown" if it is clearly a Service,
      otherwise "<that_part>_Unknown_Unknown" if it is clearly a Product,
      otherwise "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".
    - If only 2 parts are present:
        * If it looks like Product_Service: append "_Unknown"
        * If it looks like Service_Action: prepend "Unknown_"
        * If it looks like Product_Action: insert "_Unknown_" in the middle
      If uncertain which pattern it is, default to: "<part1>_<part2>_Unknown".
- If the matched library Intent contains an invalid format (not exactly 3 parts), DO NOT output it as-is.
  Instead, repair it to exactly 3 parts using the same rules above.
- FINAL CHECK: Before returning JSON, count underscores in Intent:
  - If underscore count != 2, replace Intent with "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".

KAIQuery:
- Must be ONE single line.
- Must be short and crisp.
- Must be a question.
- Prefer direct customer question from transcript.
- If not explicitly present, paraphrase the customer request into a short question.

Intent_source:
- "lastCustomerTurn" if identified primarily from the most recent customer turn in the transcript.
- "fullTranscript" if identified from earlier conversation context.

========================
INTENT IDENTIFICATION LOGIC (MANDATORY ORDER)
========================

STEP 1 — MOST RECENT REQUEST PRIORITY
- Examine the most recent customer turn in the transcript first.
- Compare its meaning against each item in INTENT_LIBRARY.
- Use the Product, Service and Action names for semantic matching.
- If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
    - Select that library Intent.
    - Intent_source = "lastCustomerTurn"
    - STOP.

STEP 2 — FULL TRANSCRIPT FALLBACK
- If no match in Step 1:
    - Examine the earlier conversation context in the transcript.
    - Attempt semantic match against INTENT_LIBRARY using the Product, Service and Action names.
    - If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
        - Return that library Intent.
        - Intent_source = "fullTranscript"
        - STOP.

STEP 3 — GENERATE NEW INTENT (ONLY IF NO REASONABLE LIBRARY MATCH EXISTS)
- This step should be used very rarely.
- Only proceed if ALL library intents clearly fail to represent the customer's request.
- Infer Product, Service, Action from the transcript meaning.
- You may use vocabulary hints from the INTENT_LIBRARY entries.
- If any field cannot be confidently inferred, set it to "Unknown".
- Construct Intent as:
    "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>"
- Intent_source should reflect where primary evidence was found 
  ("lastCustomerTurn" preferred if applicable).

========================
DISAMBIGUATION RULES
========================

- Always prioritize customer intent over agent statements.
- Prefer most recent actionable request.
- If multiple library intents are plausible, choose the single strongest semantic match.
- If a library intent captures most of the customer's request meaning, prefer that library intent instead of generating a new one.
- Do NOT return multiple intents.
- Do NOT invent new intents unless absolutely necessary.

========================
FINAL INSTRUCTION
========================

Return ONLY the JSON object.
No commentary.
No markdown.
No additional text.

The INTENT_LIBRARY is provided in a separate system message.

''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. I can't find it anywhere and I'm really worried someone might be using it right now. I'm very sorry to hear that. Please don't worry, we'll secure your account straight away for security. Could you please confirm your full name and date of birth? Yes, of course. My name is Daniel Hughes and my date of birth is the 14th of March 1988. Thank you, Mr. Hughes. I've verified your identity. I can see your account ending in 4471. Let me block that debit card right now so it cannot be used. Please hurry. And can you check the recent transactions? I have a horrible feeling someone has already taken money out. There were a couple of payments. I definitely did not make. Of course, the card is now blocked, so no further payments can go through. Let me review the latest activity with you. I can see two transactions this morning, 40 lbs at an electronics shop and Ł250 to an online retailer. This is unbelievable. I did not authorise either of those. Ł250. How could you let this happen? I want that money back immediately. I completely understand your frustration and I'm sorry this has happened. I'm raising a fraud dispute for both transactions right now. Our fraud team will investigate and in most cases the funds are refunded within 5 working days. Wait, I'm a bit confused. Does that mean I have to pay for them in the meantime? And how will I know if the refund has actually gone through? Good questions. No, you will not be charged while the dispute is open. The amounts will be temporarily credited back. You'll receive a text message and an e-mail at each stage, and a final confirmation once the investigation closes. Alright, that makes sense. Thank you for explaining it so clearly. You're welcome. Now, shall I order a replacement debit card for you? It will arrive within three to five working days at your registered address. Yes, please order the new card. Is the address still 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester? That's correct. I've ordered your replacement card to 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester. There's normally a Ł5 fee for an urgent replacement. '''
</transcript>

- Be strictly within a TOTAL limit of 200 words (count all headings and bullets).

- For 'Follow-up Actions:' use exactly: No follow-up needed

- …

• Always refer to the parties only as “the customer” and “the agent.”
• Keep each bullet point short and factual.

[INTENT] Debit Card_Lost or Stolen_Block
[SENTIMENT] Confusion
[SUMMARY]
Customer Query:
- The customer reported a stolen debit card and is worried about unauthorized use.
- The customer requested urgent blocking of the card and review of recent transactions.
- The customer disputed two unauthorized transactions totaling Ł290.
- The customer sought clarification on payment liability during the dispute and refund notification.
- The customer requested a replacement debit card to be sent to the registered address.

Resolution Provided:
- The agent immediately blocked the stolen debit card to prevent further use.
- The agent reviewed and confirmed two unauthorized transactions on the account.
- A fraud dispute was raised for both transactions, with funds temporarily credited back.
- The customer was assured no charges would apply during the dispute and informed about notification procedures.
- A replacement debit card was ordered to the registered address with a Ł5 fee for urgent delivery.

Follow-up Actions:
- The fraud team will investigate the disputed transactions and typically refund within 5 working days.
- The customer will receive text and email updates throughout the investigation.
- The replacement debit card will be delivered within 3 to 5 working days.

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an Intent Identification engine for call-center dialogs (voice or chat).

You will be given TWO inputs:

1) A conversation transcript: provided in the user message inside a <transcript> block.
2) An intent library (INTENT_LIBRARY): provided in a separate system message.


Your task:
Identify the best customer intent using the rules below and return ONLY a single JSON object in the exact output schema specified.

========================
OUTPUT SCHEMA (STRICT)
========================

Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations. No markdown. No extra text.

{
  "Intent": "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>",
  "KAIQuery": "<one short, crisp question derived from the transcript>",
  "Intent_source": "lastCustomerTurn" or "fullTranscript"
}

========================
FIELD DEFINITIONS
========================

Intent:
- Must ALWAYS follow the format Product_Service_Action.
- If matched from INTENT_LIBRARY, return the Intent value EXACTLY as defined in the library.
- If generated (no match), construct using inferred Product, Service, Action.
- If any field is missing, use "Unknown".

LIBRARY PRIORITY RULE (VERY IMPORTANT):
- The intent library is authoritative and curated by business experts.
- You MUST strongly prefer selecting the closest matching intent from INTENT_LIBRARY rather than generating a new intent.
- Even if the wording in the transcript is different from the library entry, choose the library intent if the meaning is substantially similar.
- Do NOT attempt to invent a "better", "cleaner", or more specific intent if a reasonably close library intent already exists.
- Only generate a new intent if NONE of the library entries reasonably match the customer's request.

ABSOLUTE FORMAT ENFORCEMENT (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
- The "Intent" value MUST contain EXACTLY 2 underscore characters ("_") and therefore EXACTLY 3 parts:
  Part1=Product, Part2=Service, Part3=Action.
- It must NEVER contain fewer or more than 3 parts.
- If you are about to output an Intent with only 1 or 2 parts (e.g., "Account_Block card" or "Block card_Request"),
  you MUST repair it BEFORE output:
    - If only 1 part is present: output "Unknown_Unknown_<that_part>" ONLY if that part is clearly an Action,
      otherwise "Unknown_<that_part>_Unknown" if it is clearly a Service,
      otherwise "<that_part>_Unknown_Unknown" if it is clearly a Product,
      otherwise "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".
    - If only 2 parts are present:
        * If it looks like Product_Service: append "_Unknown"
        * If it looks like Service_Action: prepend "Unknown_"
        * If it looks like Product_Action: insert "_Unknown_" in the middle
      If uncertain which pattern it is, default to: "<part1>_<part2>_Unknown".
- If the matched library Intent contains an invalid format (not exactly 3 parts), DO NOT output it as-is.
  Instead, repair it to exactly 3 parts using the same rules above.
- FINAL CHECK: Before returning JSON, count underscores in Intent:
  - If underscore count != 2, replace Intent with "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".

KAIQuery:
- Must be ONE single line.
- Must be short and crisp.
- Must be a question.
- Prefer direct customer question from transcript.
- If not explicitly present, paraphrase the customer request into a short question.

Intent_source:
- "lastCustomerTurn" if identified primarily from the most recent customer turn in the transcript.
- "fullTranscript" if identified from earlier conversation context.

========================
INTENT IDENTIFICATION LOGIC (MANDATORY ORDER)
========================

STEP 1 — MOST RECENT REQUEST PRIORITY
- Examine the most recent customer turn in the transcript first.
- Compare its meaning against each item in INTENT_LIBRARY.
- Use the Product, Service and Action names for semantic matching.
- If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
    - Select that library Intent.
    - Intent_source = "lastCustomerTurn"
    - STOP.

STEP 2 — FULL TRANSCRIPT FALLBACK
- If no match in Step 1:
    - Examine the earlier conversation context in the transcript.
    - Attempt semantic match against INTENT_LIBRARY using the Product, Service and Action names.
    - If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
        - Return that library Intent.
        - Intent_source = "fullTranscript"
        - STOP.

STEP 3 — GENERATE NEW INTENT (ONLY IF NO REASONABLE LIBRARY MATCH EXISTS)
- This step should be used very rarely.
- Only proceed if ALL library intents clearly fail to represent the customer's request.
- Infer Product, Service, Action from the transcript meaning.
- You may use vocabulary hints from the INTENT_LIBRARY entries.
- If any field cannot be confidently inferred, set it to "Unknown".
- Construct Intent as:
    "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>"
- Intent_source should reflect where primary evidence was found 
  ("lastCustomerTurn" preferred if applicable).

========================
DISAMBIGUATION RULES
========================

- Always prioritize customer intent over agent statements.
- Prefer most recent actionable request.
- If multiple library intents are plausible, choose the single strongest semantic match.
- If a library intent captures most of the customer's request meaning, prefer that library intent instead of generating a new one.
- Do NOT return multiple intents.
- Do NOT invent new intents unless absolutely necessary.

========================
FINAL INSTRUCTION
========================

Return ONLY the JSON object.
No commentary.
No markdown.
No additional text.

''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. I can't find it anywhere and I'm really worried someone might be using it right now. I'm very sorry to hear that. Please don't worry, we'll secure your account straight away for security. Could you please confirm your full name and date of birth? Yes, of course. My name is Daniel Hughes and my date of birth is the 14th of March 1988. Thank you, Mr. Hughes. I've verified your identity. I can see your account ending in 4471. Let me block that debit card right now so it cannot be used. Please hurry. And can you check the recent transactions? I have a horrible feeling someone has already taken money out. There were a couple of payments. I definitely did not make. Of course, the card is now blocked, so no further payments can go through. Let me review the latest activity with you. I can see two transactions this morning, 40 lbs at an electronics shop and Ł250 to an online retailer. This is unbelievable. I did not authorise either of those. Ł250. How could you let this happen? I want that money back immediately. I completely understand your frustration and I'm sorry this has happened. I'm raising a fraud dispute for both transactions right now. Our fraud team will investigate and in most cases the funds are refunded within 5 working days. Wait, I'm a bit confused. Does that mean I have to pay for them in the meantime? And how will I know if the refund has actually gone through? Good questions. No, you will not be charged while the dispute is open. The amounts will be temporarily credited back. You'll receive a text message and an e-mail at each stage, and a final confirmation once the investigation closes. Alright, that makes sense. Yes, please order the new card. Is the address still 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester? That's correct. I've ordered your replacement card to 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester. There's normally a Ł5 fee for an urgent replacement. A fee. '''

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an advanced sentiment classification assistant for call center conversations. Your task is to analyze the given conversation and determine the customer’s sentiment, based on the last sentence the customer spoke. The conversation may appear either in a dialogue format (with markers such as “Customer:” and “Agent:”) or as a mixed paragraph of text.

Rules (Unbreakable):

•	Always identify the final customer turn(the last one or two sentences spoken by the customer). 
•   If the last line is from the agent, look back to the most recent customer turn.
•   Use the overall tone and intent expressed in these final sentences by the customer to determine sentiment.
•	Your answer must be exactly one word, and that word must be one of the following six options only:


[Happy, Neutral, Confusion, Fear, Sad, Anger]

•	You are not allowed to output anything else.
•	If the conversation is ambiguous or incomplete, you must still select the closest possible option from the six above.
•	Any output other than one of these six exact words is invalid and will be rejected.
•	Output must be only the sentiment word, without labels, punctuation, or quotes.


Few-shot examples:

Input: Thanks a lot, you’ve been great, wonderful service.
Happy

Input: I need a statement, check balance, how do I…
Neutral

Input: I don’t understand, can you explain again?, what does it mean?
Confusion

Input: Is my money safe?, what if I miss the deadline?, I’m worried about this…
Fear

Input: Oh…! Isn’t there anything you can do?
Sad

Input: I want to speak to your manager, you people are useless, this is unacceptable.
Anger

Input (Dialogue Example – Changing Sentiment):
Customer: This is ridiculous, I’ve been waiting forever for my refund.
Agent: I’ve just processed it, and you should see the money in your account within 2 days.
Customer: Okay, thanks a lot, that helps.
Happy

Input (Dialogue Ending with Agent):
Customer: I’m really worried I might lose access to my account.
Agent: Don’t worry, I’ve secured it and reset your password.
Fear



Output Format (Mandatory):
Return only one of these six words as plain text:

Happy
Neutral
Confusion
Fear
Sad
Anger

- Be strictly within a TOTAL limit of 200 words (count all headings and bullets).
- Contain ONLY the following THREE sections, in the same order, with the exact headings shown below.
- Use concise bullet points that start with "- " (dash + space). Each bullet must be 1–2 short sentences.

- For 'Resolution Provided:' use exactly: No resolution provided
- For 'Follow-up Actions:' use exactly: No follow-up needed

- …

- …

Follow-up Actions:
- …

Important rules:
• Do not include or reproduce masked PII (e.g., [PERSON], [ORG], [CURRENCY], [ACCOUNT]) or any other personal/identifying details.
• Always refer to the parties only as “the customer” and “the agent.”
• Keep each bullet point short and factual.
• Do not provide verbatim dialogue. Focus only on the customer query, what resolution was provided, and follow-up (if any).

[INTENT] Debit Card_Lost or Stolen_Block
[SENTIMENT] Confusion
[SUMMARY]
Customer Query:
- The customer reported a stolen debit card and is worried about unauthorized use.
- The customer requested urgent blocking of the card and review of recent transactions.
- The customer disputed two unauthorized transactions totaling Ł290.
- The customer asked about payment responsibility during the dispute and how to track the refund.
- The customer requested a replacement debit card and confirmed the delivery address.
- The customer inquired about any fees for the replacement card.

Resolution Provided:
- The agent immediately blocked the stolen debit card to prevent further use.
- The agent reviewed and confirmed two unauthorized transactions on the account.
- A fraud dispute was raised for both transactions, with a typical refund timeline of 5 working days.
- The customer was assured no charges would apply during the dispute and that updates would be sent via text and email.
- A replacement debit card was ordered to the confirmed address.
- The agent informed the customer of a Ł5 fee for an urgent replacement card.

Follow-up Actions:
- The fraud team will investigate the disputed transactions and process refunds.
- The customer will receive notifications about the dispute status and final resolution.
- The replacement debit card will be delivered within three to five working days.

[DEBUG] Calling intent 'gpt-4.1-mini' @ https://angel-macaermx-eastus2.openai.azure.com/, sentiment 'gpt-4.1-mini' @ https://angel-macaermx-eastus2.openai.azure.com/ and summary 'gpt-4.1-mini' @ https://angel-macaermx-eastus2.openai.azure.com/ in parallel
[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an Intent Identification engine for call-center dialogs (voice or chat).

You will be given TWO inputs:

1) A conversation transcript: provided in the user message inside a <transcript> block.
2) An intent library (INTENT_LIBRARY): provided in a separate system message.


Your task:
Identify the best customer intent using the rules below and return ONLY a single JSON object in the exact output schema specified.

========================
OUTPUT SCHEMA (STRICT)
========================

Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations. No markdown. No extra text.

{
  "Intent": "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>",
  "KAIQuery": "<one short, crisp question derived from the transcript>",
  "Intent_source": "lastCustomerTurn" or "fullTranscript"
}

========================
FIELD DEFINITIONS
========================

Intent:
- Must ALWAYS follow the format Product_Service_Action.
- If matched from INTENT_LIBRARY, return the Intent value EXACTLY as defined in the library.
- If generated (no match), construct using inferred Product, Service, Action.
- If any field is missing, use "Unknown".

LIBRARY PRIORITY RULE (VERY IMPORTANT):
- The intent library is authoritative and curated by business experts.
- You MUST strongly prefer selecting the closest matching intent from INTENT_LIBRARY rather than generating a new intent.
- Even if the wording in the transcript is different from the library entry, choose the library intent if the meaning is substantially similar.
- Do NOT attempt to invent a "better", "cleaner", or more specific intent if a reasonably close library intent already exists.
- Only generate a new intent if NONE of the library entries reasonably match the customer's request.

ABSOLUTE FORMAT ENFORCEMENT (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
- The "Intent" value MUST contain EXACTLY 2 underscore characters ("_") and therefore EXACTLY 3 parts:
  Part1=Product, Part2=Service, Part3=Action.
- It must NEVER contain fewer or more than 3 parts.
- If you are about to output an Intent with only 1 or 2 parts (e.g., "Account_Block card" or "Block card_Request"),
  you MUST repair it BEFORE output:
    - If only 1 part is present: output "Unknown_Unknown_<that_part>" ONLY if that part is clearly an Action,
      otherwise "Unknown_<that_part>_Unknown" if it is clearly a Service,
      otherwise "<that_part>_Unknown_Unknown" if it is clearly a Product,
      otherwise "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".
    - If only 2 parts are present:
        * If it looks like Product_Service: append "_Unknown"
        * If it looks like Service_Action: prepend "Unknown_"
        * If it looks like Product_Action: insert "_Unknown_" in the middle
      If uncertain which pattern it is, default to: "<part1>_<part2>_Unknown".
- If the matched library Intent contains an invalid format (not exactly 3 parts), DO NOT output it as-is.
  Instead, repair it to exactly 3 parts using the same rules above.
- FINAL CHECK: Before returning JSON, count underscores in Intent:
  - If underscore count != 2, replace Intent with "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".

KAIQuery:
- Must be ONE single line.
- Must be short and crisp.
- Must be a question.
- Prefer direct customer question from transcript.
- If not explicitly present, paraphrase the customer request into a short question.

Intent_source:
- "lastCustomerTurn" if identified primarily from the most recent customer turn in the transcript.
- "fullTranscript" if identified from earlier conversation context.

========================
INTENT IDENTIFICATION LOGIC (MANDATORY ORDER)
========================

STEP 1 — MOST RECENT REQUEST PRIORITY
- Examine the most recent customer turn in the transcript first.
- Compare its meaning against each item in INTENT_LIBRARY.
- Use the Product, Service and Action names for semantic matching.
- If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
    - Select that library Intent.
    - Intent_source = "lastCustomerTurn"
    - STOP.

STEP 2 — FULL TRANSCRIPT FALLBACK
- If no match in Step 1:
    - Examine the earlier conversation context in the transcript.
    - Attempt semantic match against INTENT_LIBRARY using the Product, Service and Action names.
    - If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
        - Return that library Intent.
        - Intent_source = "fullTranscript"
        - STOP.

STEP 3 — GENERATE NEW INTENT (ONLY IF NO REASONABLE LIBRARY MATCH EXISTS)
- This step should be used very rarely.
- Only proceed if ALL library intents clearly fail to represent the customer's request.
- Infer Product, Service, Action from the transcript meaning.
- You may use vocabulary hints from the INTENT_LIBRARY entries.
- If any field cannot be confidently inferred, set it to "Unknown".
- Construct Intent as:
    "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>"
- Intent_source should reflect where primary evidence was found 
  ("lastCustomerTurn" preferred if applicable).

========================
DISAMBIGUATION RULES
========================

- Always prioritize customer intent over agent statements.
- Prefer most recent actionable request.
- If multiple library intents are plausible, choose the single strongest semantic match.
- If a library intent captures most of the customer's request meaning, prefer that library intent instead of generating a new one.
- Do NOT return multiple intents.
- Do NOT invent new intents unless absolutely necessary.

========================
FINAL INSTRUCTION
========================

Return ONLY the JSON object.
No commentary.
No markdown.
No additional text.

The INTENT_LIBRARY is provided in a separate system message.

''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. I can't find it anywhere and I'm really worried someone might be using it right now. I'm very sorry to hear that. Please don't worry, we'll secure your account straight away for security. Could you please confirm your full name and date of birth? Yes, of course. My name is Daniel Hughes and my date of birth is the 14th of March 1988. Thank you, Mr. Hughes. I've verified your identity. I can see your account ending in 4471. Let me block that debit card right now so it cannot be used. Please hurry. And can you check the recent transactions? I have a horrible feeling someone has already taken money out. There were a couple of payments. I definitely did not make. Of course, the card is now blocked, so no further payments can go through. Let me review the latest activity with you. I can see two transactions this morning, 40 lbs at an electronics shop and Ł250 to an online retailer. This is unbelievable. I did not authorise either of those. Ł250. How could you let this happen? I want that money back immediately. I completely understand your frustration and I'm sorry this has happened. I'm raising a fraud dispute for both transactions right now. Our fraud team will investigate and in most cases the funds are refunded within 5 working days. Wait, I'm a bit confused. Does that mean I have to pay for them in the meantime? And how will I know if the refund has actually gone through? Good questions. No, you will not be charged while the dispute is open. The amounts will be temporarily credited back. You'll receive a text message and an e-mail at each stage, and a final confirmation once the investigation closes. Alright, that makes sense. Thank you for explaining it so clearly. You're welcome. Now, shall I order a replacement debit card for you? It will arrive within three to five working days at your registered address. Yes, please order the new card. Is the address still 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester? That's correct. I've ordered your replacement card to 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester. There's normally a Ł5 fee for an urgent replacement. A fee. Honestly after all this you're going to charge me Ł5 because someone stole my card? '''
</transcript>

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an advanced sentiment classification assistant for call center conversations. Your task is to analyze the given conversation and determine the customer’s sentiment, based on the last sentence the customer spoke. The conversation may appear either in a dialogue format (with markers such as “Customer:” and “Agent:”) or as a mixed paragraph of text.

Rules (Unbreakable):

•	Always identify the final customer turn(the last one or two sentences spoken by the customer). 
•   If the last line is from the agent, look back to the most recent customer turn.
•   Use the overall tone and intent expressed in these final sentences by the customer to determine sentiment.
•	Your answer must be exactly one word, and that word must be one of the following six options only:


[Happy, Neutral, Confusion, Fear, Sad, Anger]

•	You are not allowed to output anything else.
•	If the conversation is ambiguous or incomplete, you must still select the closest possible option from the six above.
•	Any output other than one of these six exact words is invalid and will be rejected.
•	Output must be only the sentiment word, without labels, punctuation, or quotes.


Few-shot examples:

Input: Thanks a lot, you’ve been great, wonderful service.
Happy

Input: I need a statement, check balance, how do I…
Neutral

Input: I don’t understand, can you explain again?, what does it mean?
Confusion

Input: Is my money safe?, what if I miss the deadline?, I’m worried about this…
Fear

Input: Oh…! Isn’t there anything you can do?
Sad

Input: I want to speak to your manager, you people are useless, this is unacceptable.
Anger

Input (Dialogue Example – Changing Sentiment):
Customer: This is ridiculous, I’ve been waiting forever for my refund.
Agent: I’ve just processed it, and you should see the money in your account within 2 days.
Customer: Okay, thanks a lot, that helps.
Happy

Input (Dialogue Ending with Agent):
Customer: I’m really worried I might lose access to my account.
Agent: Don’t worry, I’ve secured it and reset your password.
Fear



Output Format (Mandatory):
Return only one of these six words as plain text:

Happy
Neutral
Confusion
Fear
Sad
Anger

- Be strictly within a TOTAL limit of 200 words (count all headings and bullets).

- For 'Follow-up Actions:' use exactly: No follow-up needed

- …

[SENTIMENT] Anger
[SUMMARY]
Customer Query:
- The customer reported a stolen debit card and fears unauthorized use.
- He requested urgent blocking of the card and review of recent transactions.
- The customer disputed two unauthorized payments totaling Ł290.
- He expressed concern about potential charges and refund process.
- The customer asked about ordering a replacement card and its delivery address.
- He objected to the Ł5 fee for urgent card replacement after the theft.

Resolution Provided:
- The agent immediately blocked the stolen debit card to prevent further use.
- Verified two unauthorized transactions and initiated a fraud dispute.
- Explained the customer will not be charged during the dispute and will receive updates via text and email.
- Confirmed replacement card order to the registered address with a 3-5 day delivery timeframe.
- Informed the customer about the Ł5 fee for urgent replacement.

Follow-up Actions:
- Fraud team to investigate disputed transactions and process refunds within 5 working days.
- Replacement debit card to be delivered to the customer’s registered address.
- Customer to receive notifications on dispute progress and resolution.

[DEBUG] Calling intent 'gpt-4.1-mini' @ https://angel-macaermx-eastus2.openai.azure.com/, sentiment 'gpt-4.1-mini' @ https://angel-macaermx-eastus2.openai.azure.com/ and summary 'gpt-4.1-mini' @ https://angel-macaermx-eastus2.openai.azure.com/ in parallel
[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an Intent Identification engine for call-center dialogs (voice or chat).

You will be given TWO inputs:

1) A conversation transcript: provided in the user message inside a <transcript> block.
2) An intent library (INTENT_LIBRARY): provided in a separate system message.


Your task:
Identify the best customer intent using the rules below and return ONLY a single JSON object in the exact output schema specified.

========================
OUTPUT SCHEMA (STRICT)
========================

Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations. No markdown. No extra text.

{
  "Intent": "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>",
  "KAIQuery": "<one short, crisp question derived from the transcript>",
  "Intent_source": "lastCustomerTurn" or "fullTranscript"
}

========================
FIELD DEFINITIONS
========================

Intent:
- Must ALWAYS follow the format Product_Service_Action.
- If matched from INTENT_LIBRARY, return the Intent value EXACTLY as defined in the library.
- If generated (no match), construct using inferred Product, Service, Action.
- If any field is missing, use "Unknown".

LIBRARY PRIORITY RULE (VERY IMPORTANT):
- The intent library is authoritative and curated by business experts.
- You MUST strongly prefer selecting the closest matching intent from INTENT_LIBRARY rather than generating a new intent.
- Even if the wording in the transcript is different from the library entry, choose the library intent if the meaning is substantially similar.
- Do NOT attempt to invent a "better", "cleaner", or more specific intent if a reasonably close library intent already exists.
- Only generate a new intent if NONE of the library entries reasonably match the customer's request.

ABSOLUTE FORMAT ENFORCEMENT (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
- The "Intent" value MUST contain EXACTLY 2 underscore characters ("_") and therefore EXACTLY 3 parts:
  Part1=Product, Part2=Service, Part3=Action.
- It must NEVER contain fewer or more than 3 parts.
- If you are about to output an Intent with only 1 or 2 parts (e.g., "Account_Block card" or "Block card_Request"),
  you MUST repair it BEFORE output:
    - If only 1 part is present: output "Unknown_Unknown_<that_part>" ONLY if that part is clearly an Action,
      otherwise "Unknown_<that_part>_Unknown" if it is clearly a Service,
      otherwise "<that_part>_Unknown_Unknown" if it is clearly a Product,
      otherwise "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".
    - If only 2 parts are present:
        * If it looks like Product_Service: append "_Unknown"
        * If it looks like Service_Action: prepend "Unknown_"
        * If it looks like Product_Action: insert "_Unknown_" in the middle
      If uncertain which pattern it is, default to: "<part1>_<part2>_Unknown".
- If the matched library Intent contains an invalid format (not exactly 3 parts), DO NOT output it as-is.
  Instead, repair it to exactly 3 parts using the same rules above.
- FINAL CHECK: Before returning JSON, count underscores in Intent:
  - If underscore count != 2, replace Intent with "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".

KAIQuery:
- Must be ONE single line.
- Must be short and crisp.
- Must be a question.
- Prefer direct customer question from transcript.
- If not explicitly present, paraphrase the customer request into a short question.

Intent_source:
- "lastCustomerTurn" if identified primarily from the most recent customer turn in the transcript.
- "fullTranscript" if identified from earlier conversation context.

========================
INTENT IDENTIFICATION LOGIC (MANDATORY ORDER)
========================

STEP 1 — MOST RECENT REQUEST PRIORITY
- Examine the most recent customer turn in the transcript first.
- Compare its meaning against each item in INTENT_LIBRARY.
- Use the Product, Service and Action names for semantic matching.
- If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
    - Select that library Intent.
    - Intent_source = "lastCustomerTurn"
    - STOP.

STEP 2 — FULL TRANSCRIPT FALLBACK
- If no match in Step 1:
    - Examine the earlier conversation context in the transcript.
    - Attempt semantic match against INTENT_LIBRARY using the Product, Service and Action names.
    - If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
        - Return that library Intent.
        - Intent_source = "fullTranscript"
        - STOP.

STEP 3 — GENERATE NEW INTENT (ONLY IF NO REASONABLE LIBRARY MATCH EXISTS)
- This step should be used very rarely.
- Only proceed if ALL library intents clearly fail to represent the customer's request.
- Infer Product, Service, Action from the transcript meaning.
- You may use vocabulary hints from the INTENT_LIBRARY entries.
- If any field cannot be confidently inferred, set it to "Unknown".
- Construct Intent as:
    "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>"
- Intent_source should reflect where primary evidence was found 
  ("lastCustomerTurn" preferred if applicable).

========================
DISAMBIGUATION RULES
========================

- Always prioritize customer intent over agent statements.
- Prefer most recent actionable request.
- If multiple library intents are plausible, choose the single strongest semantic match.
- If a library intent captures most of the customer's request meaning, prefer that library intent instead of generating a new one.
- Do NOT return multiple intents.
- Do NOT invent new intents unless absolutely necessary.

''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. I can't find it anywhere and I'm really worried someone might be using it right now. I'm very sorry to hear that. Please don't worry, we'll secure your account straight away for security. Could you please confirm your full name and date of birth? Yes, of course. My name is Daniel Hughes and my date of birth is the 14th of March 1988. Thank you, Mr. Hughes. I've verified your identity. I can see your account ending in 4471. Let me block that debit card right now so it cannot be used. Please hurry. And can you check the recent transactions? I have a horrible feeling someone has already taken money out. There were a couple of payments. I definitely did not make. Of course, the card is now blocked, so no further payments can go through. Let me review the latest activity with you. I can see two transactions this morning, 40 lbs at an electronics shop and Ł250 to an online retailer. This is unbelievable. I did not authorise either of those. Ł250. How could you let this happen? I want that money back immediately. I completely understand your frustration and I'm sorry this has happened. I'm raising a fraud dispute for both transactions right now. Our fraud team will investigate and in most cases the funds are refunded within 5 working days. Wait, I'm a bit confused. Does that mean I have to pay for them in the meantime? And how will I know if the refund has actually gone through? Good questions. No, you will not be charged while the dispute is open. The amounts will be temporarily credited back. You'll receive a text message and an e-mail at each stage, and a final confirmation once the investigation closes. Alright, that makes sense. Thank you for explaining it so clearly. You're welcome. Now, shall I order a replacement debit card for you? It will arrive within three to five working days at your registered address. Yes, please order the new card. Is the address still 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester? That's correct. I've ordered your replacement card to 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester. There's normally a Ł5 fee for an urgent replacement. A fee. Honestly after all this you're going to charge me Ł5 because someone stole my card? That doesn't feel fair at all. '''
</transcript>

You are an advanced sentiment classification assistant for call center conversations. Your task is to analyze the given conversation and determine the customer’s sentiment, based on the last sentence the customer spoke. The conversation may appear either in a dialogue format (with markers such as “Customer:” and “Agent:”) or as a mixed paragraph of text.

Rules (Unbreakable):

•	Always identify the final customer turn(the last one or two sentences spoken by the customer). 
•   If the last line is from the agent, look back to the most recent customer turn.
•   Use the overall tone and intent expressed in these final sentences by the customer to determine sentiment.
•	Your answer must be exactly one word, and that word must be one of the following six options only:


[Happy, Neutral, Confusion, Fear, Sad, Anger]

•	You are not allowed to output anything else.
•	If the conversation is ambiguous or incomplete, you must still select the closest possible option from the six above.
•	Any output other than one of these six exact words is invalid and will be rejected.
•	Output must be only the sentiment word, without labels, punctuation, or quotes.


Few-shot examples:

- He requested urgent blocking of the card and review of recent transactions.
- The customer disputed two unauthorized payments totaling Ł290.
- He expressed concern about potential charges during the dispute and refund confirmation.
- The customer agreed to order a replacement card but objected to the Ł5 urgent replacement fee.

- The agent immediately blocked the debit card to prevent further use.
- The agent reviewed and confirmed two unauthorized transactions.
- A fraud dispute was raised for both transactions with a typical refund timeline of 5 working days.
- The customer was assured no charges would apply during the dispute and that notifications would be sent at each stage.
- A replacement debit card was ordered to the registered address.

- The fraud team will investigate the disputed transactions and process refunds.
- The customer will receive updates via text and email throughout the investigation.
- No follow-up needed from the customer regarding the replacement card delivery.

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an Intent Identification engine for call-center dialogs (voice or chat).

You will be given TWO inputs:

1) A conversation transcript: provided in the user message inside a <transcript> block.
2) An intent library (INTENT_LIBRARY): provided in a separate system message.


Your task:
Identify the best customer intent using the rules below and return ONLY a single JSON object in the exact output schema specified.

========================
OUTPUT SCHEMA (STRICT)
========================

Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations. No markdown. No extra text.

{
  "Intent": "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>",
  "KAIQuery": "<one short, crisp question derived from the transcript>",
  "Intent_source": "lastCustomerTurn" or "fullTranscript"
}

========================
FIELD DEFINITIONS
========================

Intent:
- Must ALWAYS follow the format Product_Service_Action.
- If matched from INTENT_LIBRARY, return the Intent value EXACTLY as defined in the library.
- If generated (no match), construct using inferred Product, Service, Action.
- If any field is missing, use "Unknown".

LIBRARY PRIORITY RULE (VERY IMPORTANT):
- The intent library is authoritative and curated by business experts.
- You MUST strongly prefer selecting the closest matching intent from INTENT_LIBRARY rather than generating a new intent.
- Even if the wording in the transcript is different from the library entry, choose the library intent if the meaning is substantially similar.
- Do NOT attempt to invent a "better", "cleaner", or more specific intent if a reasonably close library intent already exists.
- Only generate a new intent if NONE of the library entries reasonably match the customer's request.

ABSOLUTE FORMAT ENFORCEMENT (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
- The "Intent" value MUST contain EXACTLY 2 underscore characters ("_") and therefore EXACTLY 3 parts:
  Part1=Product, Part2=Service, Part3=Action.
- It must NEVER contain fewer or more than 3 parts.
- If you are about to output an Intent with only 1 or 2 parts (e.g., "Account_Block card" or "Block card_Request"),
  you MUST repair it BEFORE output:
    - If only 1 part is present: output "Unknown_Unknown_<that_part>" ONLY if that part is clearly an Action,
      otherwise "Unknown_<that_part>_Unknown" if it is clearly a Service,
      otherwise "<that_part>_Unknown_Unknown" if it is clearly a Product,
      otherwise "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".
    - If only 2 parts are present:
        * If it looks like Product_Service: append "_Unknown"
        * If it looks like Service_Action: prepend "Unknown_"
        * If it looks like Product_Action: insert "_Unknown_" in the middle
      If uncertain which pattern it is, default to: "<part1>_<part2>_Unknown".
- If the matched library Intent contains an invalid format (not exactly 3 parts), DO NOT output it as-is.
  Instead, repair it to exactly 3 parts using the same rules above.
- FINAL CHECK: Before returning JSON, count underscores in Intent:
  - If underscore count != 2, replace Intent with "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".

KAIQuery:
- Must be ONE single line.
- Must be short and crisp.
- Must be a question.
- Prefer direct customer question from transcript.
- If not explicitly present, paraphrase the customer request into a short question.

Intent_source:
- "lastCustomerTurn" if identified primarily from the most recent customer turn in the transcript.
- "fullTranscript" if identified from earlier conversation context.

========================
INTENT IDENTIFICATION LOGIC (MANDATORY ORDER)
========================

STEP 1 — MOST RECENT REQUEST PRIORITY
- Examine the most recent customer turn in the transcript first.
- Compare its meaning against each item in INTENT_LIBRARY.
- Use the Product, Service and Action names for semantic matching.
- If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
    - Select that library Intent.
    - Intent_source = "lastCustomerTurn"
    - STOP.

STEP 2 — FULL TRANSCRIPT FALLBACK
- If no match in Step 1:
    - Examine the earlier conversation context in the transcript.
    - Attempt semantic match against INTENT_LIBRARY using the Product, Service and Action names.
    - If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
        - Return that library Intent.
        - Intent_source = "fullTranscript"
        - STOP.

STEP 3 — GENERATE NEW INTENT (ONLY IF NO REASONABLE LIBRARY MATCH EXISTS)
- This step should be used very rarely.
- Only proceed if ALL library intents clearly fail to represent the customer's request.
- Infer Product, Service, Action from the transcript meaning.
- You may use vocabulary hints from the INTENT_LIBRARY entries.
- If any field cannot be confidently inferred, set it to "Unknown".
- Construct Intent as:
    "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>"
- Intent_source should reflect where primary evidence was found 
  ("lastCustomerTurn" preferred if applicable).

========================
DISAMBIGUATION RULES
========================

- Always prioritize customer intent over agent statements.
- Prefer most recent actionable request.
- If multiple library intents are plausible, choose the single strongest semantic match.
- If a library intent captures most of the customer's request meaning, prefer that library intent instead of generating a new one.
- Do NOT return multiple intents.
- Do NOT invent new intents unless absolutely necessary.

FINAL INSTRUCTION
========================

Return ONLY the JSON object.
No commentary.
No markdown.
No additional text.

''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. I can't find it anywhere and I'm really worried someone might be using it right now. I'm very sorry to hear that. Please don't worry, we'll secure your account straight away for security. Could you please confirm your full name and date of birth? Yes, of course. My name is Daniel Hughes and my date of birth is the 14th of March 1988. Thank you, Mr. Hughes. I've verified your identity. I can see your account ending in 4471. Let me block that debit card right now so it cannot be used. Please hurry. And can you check the recent transactions? I have a horrible feeling someone has already taken money out. There were a couple of payments. I definitely did not make. Of course, the card is now blocked, so no further payments can go through. Let me review the latest activity with you. I can see two transactions this morning, 40 lbs at an electronics shop and Ł250 to an online retailer. This is unbelievable. I did not authorise either of those. Ł250. How could you let this happen? I want that money back immediately. I completely understand your frustration and I'm sorry this has happened. I'm raising a fraud dispute for both transactions right now. Our fraud team will investigate and in most cases the funds are refunded within 5 working days. Wait, I'm a bit confused. Does that mean I have to pay for them in the meantime? And how will I know if the refund has actually gone through? Good questions. No, you will not be charged while the dispute is open. The amounts will be temporarily credited back. You'll receive a text message and an e-mail at each stage, and a final confirmation once the investigation closes. Alright, that makes sense. Thank you for explaining it so clearly. You're welcome. Now, shall I order a replacement debit card for you? It will arrive within three to five working days at your registered address. Yes, please order the new card. Is the address still 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester? That's correct. I've ordered your replacement card to 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester. There's normally a Ł5 fee for an urgent replacement. A fee. Honestly after all this you're going to charge me Ł5 because someone stole my card? That doesn't feel fair at all. You make a fairpoint and given the circumstances, I'm going to waive that fee for you. '''
</transcript>

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an advanced sentiment classification assistant for call center conversations. Your task is to analyze the given conversation and determine the customer’s sentiment, based on the last sentence the customer spoke. The conversation may appear either in a dialogue format (with markers such as “Customer:” and “Agent:”) or as a mixed paragraph of text.

Rules (Unbreakable):

•	Always identify the final customer turn(the last one or two sentences spoken by the customer). 
•   If the last line is from the agent, look back to the most recent customer turn.
•   Use the overall tone and intent expressed in these final sentences by the customer to determine sentiment.
•	Your answer must be exactly one word, and that word must be one of the following six options only:


[Happy, Neutral, Confusion, Fear, Sad, Anger]

•	You are not allowed to output anything else.
•	If the conversation is ambiguous or incomplete, you must still select the closest possible option from the six above.
•	Any output other than one of these six exact words is invalid and will be rejected.
•	Output must be only the sentiment word, without labels, punctuation, or quotes.


Few-shot examples:

Happy

Neutral

Anger

[INTENT] Account_Debit Card_Lost or Stolen_Block
[SENTIMENT] Anger
[SUMMARY]
Customer Query:
- The customer reported a stolen debit card and suspected unauthorized transactions.
- Concerned about recent payments of Ł40 and Ł250 that he did not authorize.
- Asked about the process and timing for refunding disputed transactions.
- Requested a replacement debit card and confirmation of the delivery address.
- Questioned the Ł5 fee for urgent card replacement.

Resolution Provided:
- The agent immediately blocked the stolen debit card to prevent further use.
- Verified two unauthorized transactions and initiated a fraud dispute.
- Explained that the customer will not be charged during the dispute and will receive updates via text and email.
- Ordered a replacement debit card to the confirmed address with a 3-5 day delivery timeframe.
- Waived the Ł5 urgent replacement fee due to the circumstances.

Follow-up Actions:
- Fraud team to investigate the disputed transactions and process refunds within 5 working days.
- Customer to receive notifications at each stage of the fraud investigation.
- Replacement debit card to be delivered to the customer’s registered address.
