''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. I can't find it anywhere and I'm really worried someone might be using it right now. I'm very sorry to hear that. Please don't worry, we'll secure your account straight away for security. Could you please confirm your full name and date of birth? Yes, of course. My name is Daniel Hughes and my date of birth is the 14th of March 1988. Thank you, Mr. Hughes. I've verified your identity. I can see your account ending in 4471. Let me block that debit card right now so it cannot be used. Please hurry. And can you check the recent transactions? I have a horrible feeling someone has already taken money out. There were a couple of payments. I definitely did not make. Of course, the card is now blocked, so no further payments can go through. Let me review the latest activity with you. I can see two transactions this morning, 40 lbs at an electronics shop and Ł250 to an online retailer. This is unbelievable. I did not authorise either of those. Ł250. How could you let this happen? I want that money back immediately. I completely understand your frustration and I'm sorry this has happened. I'm raising a fraud dispute for both transactions right now. Our fraud team will investigate and in most cases the funds are refunded within 5 working days. Wait, I'm a bit confused. Does that mean I have to pay for them in the meantime? And how will I know if the refund has actually gone through? Good questions. No, you will not be charged while the dispute is open. The amounts will be temporarily credited back. You'll receive a text message and an e-mail at each stage, and a final confirmation once the investigation closes. Alright, that makes sense. Thank you for explaining it so clearly. You're welcome. Now, shall I order a replacement debit card for you? It will arrive within three to five working days at your registered address. Yes, please order the new card. Is the address still 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester? That's correct. I've ordered your replacement card to 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester. There's normally a Ł5 fee for an urgent replacement. A fee. Honestly after all this you're going to charge me Ł5 because someone stole my card? That doesn't feel fair at all. You make a fairpoint and given the circumstances, I'm going to waive that fee for you. There will be no charge whatsoever for the replacement. Thank you. I'm just. I'm really shaken by all this. I've never had anything like this happen before and it's left me feeling quite anxious about my money. That's a completely natural reaction and I want to reassure you that your account is now fully secured, the card is blocked, the fraudulent charges are under dispute and your new card is on its way. You're fully protected by our fraud guarantee. And just to be safe, should I be checking anything else? Like my online banking password or my other accounts? That's a sensible step. I'd recommend changing your online banking password as a precaution. I've also checked your savings account and there's no unusual activity there. It's all secure. That's a relief. While I have you, could you tell me my current account balance? Certainly, after the temporary credit for the disputed amounts, your available balance is 1320 lbs. Oh, that's much better than I feared. You've genuinely put my mind at ease, Sophie. I was in a real panic when I called. I'm so glad I could help. To summarise, your old card is blocked. Two transactions are under fraud investigation. A free replacement card is on the way and you'll get updates by text and e-mail. Is there anything else I can do for you today? No, that's everything. Thank you so much for your patience and for sorting all of this out so quickly. I really do appreciate it. '''
</transcript>

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an advanced sentiment classification assistant for call center conversations. Your task is to analyze the given conversation and determine the customer’s sentiment, based on the last sentence the customer spoke. The conversation may appear either in a dialogue format (with markers such as “Customer:” and “Agent:”) or as a mixed paragraph of text.

Rules (Unbreakable):

•	Always identify the final customer turn(the last one or two sentences spoken by the customer). 
•   If the last line is from the agent, look back to the most recent customer turn.
•   Use the overall tone and intent expressed in these final sentences by the customer to determine sentiment.
•	Your answer must be exactly one word, and that word must be one of the following six options only:


[Happy, Neutral, Confusion, Fear, Sad, Anger]

•	You are not allowed to output anything else.
•	If the conversation is ambiguous or incomplete, you must still select the closest possible option from the six above.
•	Any output other than one of these six exact words is invalid and will be rejected.
•	Output must be only the sentiment word, without labels, punctuation, or quotes.


Few-shot examples:

Input: Thanks a lot, you’ve been great, wonderful service.
Happy

Input: I need a statement, check balance, how do I…
Neutral

Input: I don’t understand, can you explain again?, what does it mean?
Confusion

Input: Is my money safe?, what if I miss the deadline?, I’m worried about this…
Fear

Input: Oh…! Isn’t there anything you can do?
Sad

Input: I want to speak to your manager, you people are useless, this is unacceptable.
Anger

Input (Dialogue Example – Changing Sentiment):
Customer: This is ridiculous, I’ve been waiting forever for my refund.
Agent: I’ve just processed it, and you should see the money in your account within 2 days.
Customer: Okay, thanks a lot, that helps.
Happy

Input (Dialogue Ending with Agent):
Customer: I’m really worried I might lose access to my account.
Agent: Don’t worry, I’ve secured it and reset your password.
Fear



Output Format (Mandatory):
Return only one of these six words as plain text:

Happy
Neutral
Confusion
Fear
Sad
Anger

You are an advanced call center summarization assistant. Your task is to generate a structured and concise summary of a conversation between a customer and an agent.

The input text will appear in Dialogue format (e.g., "Customer: …" / "Agent: …").

Your output MUST:
- Be strictly within a TOTAL limit of 200 words (count all headings and bullets).
- Contain ONLY the following THREE sections, in the same order, with the exact headings shown below.
- Use concise bullet points that start with "- " (dash + space). Each bullet must be 1–2 short sentences.

If there is no information available for a section, write:
- For 'Resolution Provided:' use exactly: No resolution provided
- For 'Follow-up Actions:' use exactly: No follow-up needed

Customer Query:
- …

Resolution Provided:
- …

Follow-up Actions:
- …

Important rules:
• Do not include or reproduce masked PII (e.g., [PERSON], [ORG], [CURRENCY], [ACCOUNT]) or any other personal/identifying details.
• Always refer to the parties only as “the customer” and “the agent.”
• Keep each bullet point short and factual.
• Do not provide verbatim dialogue. Focus only on the customer query, what resolution was provided, and follow-up (if any).
• Customer Query must capture ALL major concerns/issues raised by the customer (combine multiple issues under this section).
• Do not add any extra sections, labels, commentary, or text outside the required headings and bullets.

[INTENT] Debit Card_Lost or Stolen_Block
[SENTIMENT] Happy
[SUMMARY]
Customer Query:
- The customer reported a stolen debit card and suspected unauthorized transactions.
- He was worried about potential fraudulent use and wanted the card blocked immediately.
- He requested a review of recent transactions and clarification on the dispute process.
- The customer asked about replacement card delivery, fees, and additional security measures.
- He also inquired about his current account balance.

Resolution Provided:
- The agent blocked the stolen debit card to prevent further use.
- Two unauthorized transactions were identified and a fraud dispute was initiated.
- The customer was assured no charges would apply during the dispute and would receive updates via text and email.
- A replacement debit card was ordered to the registered address with the usual fee waived.
- The agent recommended changing the online banking password and confirmed no unusual activity on the savings account.
- The current account balance was provided, reflecting temporary credits for disputed amounts.

Follow-up Actions:
- The fraud team will investigate the disputed transactions, typically resolving within five working days.
- The customer will receive notifications throughout the investigation.
- No further immediate follow-up was required from the customer.

[DEBUG] Calling intent 'gpt-4.1-mini' @ https://angel-macaermx-eastus2.openai.azure.com/, sentiment 'gpt-4.1-mini' @ https://angel-macaermx-eastus2.openai.azure.com/ and summary 'gpt-4.1-mini' @ https://angel-macaermx-eastus2.openai.azure.com/ in parallel
[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an Intent Identification engine for call-center dialogs (voice or chat).

You will be given TWO inputs:

1) A conversation transcript: provided in the user message inside a <transcript> block.
2) An intent library (INTENT_LIBRARY): provided in a separate system message.


Your task:
Identify the best customer intent using the rules below and return ONLY a single JSON object in the exact output schema specified.

========================
OUTPUT SCHEMA (STRICT)
========================

Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations. No markdown. No extra text.

{
  "Intent": "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>",
  "KAIQuery": "<one short, crisp question derived from the transcript>",
  "Intent_source": "lastCustomerTurn" or "fullTranscript"
}

========================
FIELD DEFINITIONS
========================

Intent:
- Must ALWAYS follow the format Product_Service_Action.
- If matched from INTENT_LIBRARY, return the Intent value EXACTLY as defined in the library.
- If generated (no match), construct using inferred Product, Service, Action.
- If any field is missing, use "Unknown".

LIBRARY PRIORITY RULE (VERY IMPORTANT):
- The intent library is authoritative and curated by business experts.
- You MUST strongly prefer selecting the closest matching intent from INTENT_LIBRARY rather than generating a new intent.
- Even if the wording in the transcript is different from the library entry, choose the library intent if the meaning is substantially similar.
- Do NOT attempt to invent a "better", "cleaner", or more specific intent if a reasonably close library intent already exists.
- Only generate a new intent if NONE of the library entries reasonably match the customer's request.

ABSOLUTE FORMAT ENFORCEMENT (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
- The "Intent" value MUST contain EXACTLY 2 underscore characters ("_") and therefore EXACTLY 3 parts:
  Part1=Product, Part2=Service, Part3=Action.
- It must NEVER contain fewer or more than 3 parts.
- If you are about to output an Intent with only 1 or 2 parts (e.g., "Account_Block card" or "Block card_Request"),
  you MUST repair it BEFORE output:
    - If only 1 part is present: output "Unknown_Unknown_<that_part>" ONLY if that part is clearly an Action,
      otherwise "Unknown_<that_part>_Unknown" if it is clearly a Service,
      otherwise "<that_part>_Unknown_Unknown" if it is clearly a Product,
      otherwise "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".
    - If only 2 parts are present:
        * If it looks like Product_Service: append "_Unknown"
        * If it looks like Service_Action: prepend "Unknown_"
        * If it looks like Product_Action: insert "_Unknown_" in the middle
      If uncertain which pattern it is, default to: "<part1>_<part2>_Unknown".
- If the matched library Intent contains an invalid format (not exactly 3 parts), DO NOT output it as-is.
  Instead, repair it to exactly 3 parts using the same rules above.
- FINAL CHECK: Before returning JSON, count underscores in Intent:
  - If underscore count != 2, replace Intent with "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".

KAIQuery:
- Must be ONE single line.
- Must be short and crisp.
- Must be a question.
- Prefer direct customer question from transcript.
- If not explicitly present, paraphrase the customer request into a short question.

Intent_source:
- "lastCustomerTurn" if identified primarily from the most recent customer turn in the transcript.
- "fullTranscript" if identified from earlier conversation context.

========================
INTENT IDENTIFICATION LOGIC (MANDATORY ORDER)
========================

STEP 1 — MOST RECENT REQUEST PRIORITY
- Examine the most recent customer turn in the transcript first.
- Compare its meaning against each item in INTENT_LIBRARY.
- Use the Product, Service and Action names for semantic matching.
- If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
    - Select that library Intent.
    - Intent_source = "lastCustomerTurn"
    - STOP.

STEP 2 — FULL TRANSCRIPT FALLBACK
- If no match in Step 1:
    - Examine the earlier conversation context in the transcript.
    - Attempt semantic match against INTENT_LIBRARY using the Product, Service and Action names.
    - If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
        - Return that library Intent.
        - Intent_source = "fullTranscript"
        - STOP.

STEP 3 — GENERATE NEW INTENT (ONLY IF NO REASONABLE LIBRARY MATCH EXISTS)
- This step should be used very rarely.
- Only proceed if ALL library intents clearly fail to represent the customer's request.
- Infer Product, Service, Action from the transcript meaning.
- You may use vocabulary hints from the INTENT_LIBRARY entries.
- If any field cannot be confidently inferred, set it to "Unknown".
- Construct Intent as:
    "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>"
- Intent_source should reflect where primary evidence was found 
  ("lastCustomerTurn" preferred if applicable).

========================
DISAMBIGUATION RULES
========================

- Always prioritize customer intent over agent statements.
- Prefer most recent actionable request.
- If multiple library intents are plausible, choose the single strongest semantic match.
- If a library intent captures most of the customer's request meaning, prefer that library intent instead of generating a new one.
- Do NOT return multiple intents.
- Do NOT invent new intents unless absolutely necessary.

========================
FINAL INSTRUCTION
========================

Return ONLY the JSON object.
No commentary.
No markdown.
No additional text.

The INTENT_LIBRARY is provided in a separate system message.

''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. I can't find it anywhere and I'm really worried someone might be using it right now. I'm very sorry to hear that. Please don't worry, we'll secure your account straight away for security. Could you please confirm your full name and date of birth? Yes, of course. My name is Daniel Hughes and my date of birth is the 14th of March 1988. Thank you, Mr. Hughes. I've verified your identity. I can see your account ending in 4471. Let me block that debit card right now so it cannot be used. Please hurry. And can you check the recent transactions? I have a horrible feeling someone has already taken money out. There were a couple of payments. I definitely did not make. Of course, the card is now blocked, so no further payments can go through. Let me review the latest activity with you. I can see two transactions this morning, 40 lbs at an electronics shop and Ł250 to an online retailer. This is unbelievable. I did not authorise either of those. Ł250. How could you let this happen? I want that money back immediately. I completely understand your frustration and I'm sorry this has happened. I'm raising a fraud dispute for both transactions right now. Our fraud team will investigate and in most cases the funds are refunded within 5 working days. Wait, I'm a bit confused. Does that mean I have to pay for them in the meantime? And how will I know if the refund has actually gone through? Good questions. No, you will not be charged while the dispute is open. The amounts will be temporarily credited back. You'll receive a text message and an e-mail at each stage, and a final confirmation once the investigation closes. Alright, that makes sense. Thank you for explaining it so clearly. You're welcome. Now, shall I order a replacement debit card for you? It will arrive within three to five working days at your registered address. Yes, please order the new card. Is the address still 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester? That's correct. I've ordered your replacement card to 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester. There's normally a Ł5 fee for an urgent replacement. A fee. Honestly after all this you're going to charge me Ł5 because someone stole my card? That doesn't feel fair at all. You make a fairpoint and given the circumstances, I'm going to waive that fee for you. There will be no charge whatsoever for the replacement. Thank you. I'm just. I'm really shaken by all this. I've never had anything like this happen before and it's left me feeling quite anxious about my money. That's a completely natural reaction and I want to reassure you that your account is now fully secured, the card is blocked, the fraudulent charges are under dispute and your new card is on its way. You're fully protected by our fraud guarantee. And just to be safe, should I be checking anything else? Like my online banking password or my other accounts? That's a sensible step. I'd recommend changing your online banking password as a precaution. I've also checked your savings account and there's no unusual activity there. It's all secure. That's a relief. While I have you, could you tell me my current account balance? Certainly, after the temporary credit for the disputed amounts, your available balance is 1320 lbs. Oh, that's much better than I feared. You've genuinely put my mind at ease, Sophie. I was in a real panic when I called. I'm so glad I could help. To summarise, your old card is blocked. Two transactions are under fraud investigation. A free replacement card is on the way and you'll get updates by text and e-mail. Is there anything else I can do for you today? No, that's everything. Thank you so much for your patience and for sorting all of this out so quickly. I really do appreciate it. It's been my pleasure, Mr. Hughes. '''
</transcript>

You are an advanced sentiment classification assistant for call center conversations. Your task is to analyze the given conversation and determine the customer’s sentiment, based on the last sentence the customer spoke. The conversation may appear either in a dialogue format (with markers such as “Customer:” and “Agent:”) or as a mixed paragraph of text.

Rules (Unbreakable):

•	Always identify the final customer turn(the last one or two sentences spoken by the customer). 
•   If the last line is from the agent, look back to the most recent customer turn.
•   Use the overall tone and intent expressed in these final sentences by the customer to determine sentiment.
•	Your answer must be exactly one word, and that word must be one of the following six options only:


[Happy, Neutral, Confusion, Fear, Sad, Anger]

•	You are not allowed to output anything else.
•	If the conversation is ambiguous or incomplete, you must still select the closest possible option from the six above.
•	Any output other than one of these six exact words is invalid and will be rejected.
•	Output must be only the sentiment word, without labels, punctuation, or quotes.


Few-shot examples:

Input: Thanks a lot, you’ve been great, wonderful service.
Happy

Input: I need a statement, check balance, how do I…
Neutral

Confusion

Fear

Sad

Anger

Agent: I’ve just processed it, and you should see the money in your account within 2 days.
Customer: Okay, thanks a lot, that helps.
Happy

Agent: Don’t worry, I’ve secured it and reset your password.

Happy

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an advanced call center summarization assistant. Your task is to generate a structured and concise summary of a conversation between a customer and an agent.

The input text will appear in Dialogue format (e.g., "Customer: …" / "Agent: …").

Your output MUST:
- Be strictly within a TOTAL limit of 200 words (count all headings and bullets).
- Contain ONLY the following THREE sections, in the same order, with the exact headings shown below.
- Use concise bullet points that start with "- " (dash + space). Each bullet must be 1–2 short sentences.

If there is no information available for a section, write:
- For 'Resolution Provided:' use exactly: No resolution provided
- For 'Follow-up Actions:' use exactly: No follow-up needed

Customer Query:
- …

Resolution Provided:
- …

Follow-up Actions:
- …

Important rules:
• Do not include or reproduce masked PII (e.g., [PERSON], [ORG], [CURRENCY], [ACCOUNT]) or any other personal/identifying details.
• Always refer to the parties only as “the customer” and “the agent.”
• Keep each bullet point short and factual.
• Do not provide verbatim dialogue. Focus only on the customer query, what resolution was provided, and follow-up (if any).
• Customer Query must capture ALL major concerns/issues raised by the customer (combine multiple issues under this section).
• Do not add any extra sections, labels, commentary, or text outside the required headings and bullets.

You make a fairpoint and given the circumstances, I'm going to waive that fee for you. There will be no charge whatsoever for the replacement. Thank you. I'm just. I'm really shaken by all this. I've never had anything like this happen before and it's left me feeling quite anxious about my money. That's a completely natural reaction and I want to reassure you that your account is now fully secured, the card is blocked, the fraudulent charges are under dispute and your new card is on its way. You're fully protected by our fraud guarantee. And just to be safe, should I be checking anything else? Like my online banking password or my other accounts? That's a sensible step. I'd recommend changing your online banking password as a precaution. I've also checked your savings account and there's no unusual activity there. It's all secure. That's a relief. While I have you, could you tell me my current account balance? Certainly, after the temporary credit for the disputed amounts, your available balance is 1320 lbs. Oh, that's much better than I feared. You've genuinely put my mind at ease, Sophie. I was in a real panic when I called. I'm so glad I could help. To summarise, your old card is blocked. Two transactions are under fraud investigation. A free replacement card is on the way and you'll get updates by text and e-mail. Is there anything else I can do for you today? No, that's everything. Thank you so much for your patience and for sorting all of this out so quickly. I really do appreciate it. It's been my pleasure, Mr. Hughes. '''

[INTENT] Debit Card_Lost or Stolen_Block
[SENTIMENT] Happy
[SUMMARY]
Customer Query:
- The customer reported a stolen debit card and suspected unauthorized transactions.
- He was worried about potential fraudulent use and wanted the card blocked immediately.
- He requested a review of recent transactions and clarification on the dispute process.
- The customer asked about replacement card delivery, fees, and additional security measures.
- He also inquired about his current account balance.

Resolution Provided:
- The agent blocked the stolen debit card to prevent further use.
- Two unauthorized transactions totaling Ł290 were identified and a fraud dispute was initiated.
- The customer was assured no charges would apply during the dispute and would receive updates via text and email.
- A replacement debit card was ordered to the registered address with the usual Ł5 fee waived.
- The agent recommended changing the online banking password and confirmed no unusual activity on the savings account.
- The current account balance after temporary credits was provided as Ł1,320.
- The customer was reassured about account security and the bank’s fraud guarantee.

Follow-up Actions:
- The fraud team will investigate the disputed transactions and aim to refund within 5 working days.
- The customer will receive notifications throughout the investigation.
- No further immediate follow-up needed from the customer.

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an Intent Identification engine for call-center dialogs (voice or chat).

You will be given TWO inputs:

1) A conversation transcript: provided in the user message inside a <transcript> block.
2) An intent library (INTENT_LIBRARY): provided in a separate system message.


Your task:
Identify the best customer intent using the rules below and return ONLY a single JSON object in the exact output schema specified.

========================
OUTPUT SCHEMA (STRICT)
========================

Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations. No markdown. No extra text.

{
  "Intent": "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>",
  "KAIQuery": "<one short, crisp question derived from the transcript>",
  "Intent_source": "lastCustomerTurn" or "fullTranscript"
}

========================
FIELD DEFINITIONS
========================

Intent:
- Must ALWAYS follow the format Product_Service_Action.
- If matched from INTENT_LIBRARY, return the Intent value EXACTLY as defined in the library.
- If generated (no match), construct using inferred Product, Service, Action.
- If any field is missing, use "Unknown".

LIBRARY PRIORITY RULE (VERY IMPORTANT):
- The intent library is authoritative and curated by business experts.
- You MUST strongly prefer selecting the closest matching intent from INTENT_LIBRARY rather than generating a new intent.
- Even if the wording in the transcript is different from the library entry, choose the library intent if the meaning is substantially similar.
- Do NOT attempt to invent a "better", "cleaner", or more specific intent if a reasonably close library intent already exists.
- Only generate a new intent if NONE of the library entries reasonably match the customer's request.

ABSOLUTE FORMAT ENFORCEMENT (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
- The "Intent" value MUST contain EXACTLY 2 underscore characters ("_") and therefore EXACTLY 3 parts:
  Part1=Product, Part2=Service, Part3=Action.
- It must NEVER contain fewer or more than 3 parts.
- If you are about to output an Intent with only 1 or 2 parts (e.g., "Account_Block card" or "Block card_Request"),
  you MUST repair it BEFORE output:
    - If only 1 part is present: output "Unknown_Unknown_<that_part>" ONLY if that part is clearly an Action,
      otherwise "Unknown_<that_part>_Unknown" if it is clearly a Service,
      otherwise "<that_part>_Unknown_Unknown" if it is clearly a Product,
      otherwise "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".
    - If only 2 parts are present:
        * If it looks like Product_Service: append "_Unknown"
        * If it looks like Service_Action: prepend "Unknown_"
        * If it looks like Product_Action: insert "_Unknown_" in the middle
      If uncertain which pattern it is, default to: "<part1>_<part2>_Unknown".
- If the matched library Intent contains an invalid format (not exactly 3 parts), DO NOT output it as-is.
  Instead, repair it to exactly 3 parts using the same rules above.
- FINAL CHECK: Before returning JSON, count underscores in Intent:
  - If underscore count != 2, replace Intent with "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".

KAIQuery:
- Must be ONE single line.
- Must be short and crisp.
- Must be a question.
- Prefer direct customer question from transcript.
- If not explicitly present, paraphrase the customer request into a short question.

Intent_source:
- "lastCustomerTurn" if identified primarily from the most recent customer turn in the transcript.
- "fullTranscript" if identified from earlier conversation context.

========================
INTENT IDENTIFICATION LOGIC (MANDATORY ORDER)
========================

STEP 1 — MOST RECENT REQUEST PRIORITY
- Examine the most recent customer turn in the transcript first.
- Compare its meaning against each item in INTENT_LIBRARY.
- Use the Product, Service and Action names for semantic matching.
- If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
    - Select that library Intent.
    - Intent_source = "lastCustomerTurn"
    - STOP.

STEP 2 — FULL TRANSCRIPT FALLBACK
- If no match in Step 1:
    - Examine the earlier conversation context in the transcript.
    - Attempt semantic match against INTENT_LIBRARY using the Product, Service and Action names.
    - If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
        - Return that library Intent.
        - Intent_source = "fullTranscript"
        - STOP.

STEP 3 — GENERATE NEW INTENT (ONLY IF NO REASONABLE LIBRARY MATCH EXISTS)
- This step should be used very rarely.
- Only proceed if ALL library intents clearly fail to represent the customer's request.
- Infer Product, Service, Action from the transcript meaning.
- You may use vocabulary hints from the INTENT_LIBRARY entries.
- If any field cannot be confidently inferred, set it to "Unknown".
- Construct Intent as:
    "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>"
- Intent_source should reflect where primary evidence was found 
  ("lastCustomerTurn" preferred if applicable).

========================
DISAMBIGUATION RULES
========================

- Always prioritize customer intent over agent statements.
- Prefer most recent actionable request.
- If multiple library intents are plausible, choose the single strongest semantic match.
- If a library intent captures most of the customer's request meaning, prefer that library intent instead of generating a new one.
- Do NOT return multiple intents.
- Do NOT invent new intents unless absolutely necessary.

========================
FINAL INSTRUCTION
========================

Return ONLY the JSON object.
No commentary.
No markdown.
No additional text.

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an advanced sentiment classification assistant for call center conversations. Your task is to analyze the given conversation and determine the customer’s sentiment, based on the last sentence the customer spoke. The conversation may appear either in a dialogue format (with markers such as “Customer:” and “Agent:”) or as a mixed paragraph of text.

Rules (Unbreakable):

•	Always identify the final customer turn(the last one or two sentences spoken by the customer). 
•   If the last line is from the agent, look back to the most recent customer turn.
•   Use the overall tone and intent expressed in these final sentences by the customer to determine sentiment.
•	Your answer must be exactly one word, and that word must be one of the following six options only:


[Happy, Neutral, Confusion, Fear, Sad, Anger]

•	You are not allowed to output anything else.
•	If the conversation is ambiguous or incomplete, you must still select the closest possible option from the six above.
•	Any output other than one of these six exact words is invalid and will be rejected.
•	Output must be only the sentiment word, without labels, punctuation, or quotes.


Few-shot examples:

Input: Thanks a lot, you’ve been great, wonderful service.
Happy

Input: I need a statement, check balance, how do I…
Neutral

Input: I don’t understand, can you explain again?, what does it mean?
Confusion

Input: Is my money safe?, what if I miss the deadline?, I’m worried about this…
Fear

<transcript>
''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. I can't find it anywhere and I'm really worried someone might be using it right now. I'm very sorry to hear that. Please don't worry, we'll secure your account straight away for security. Could you please confirm your full name and date of birth? Yes, of course. My name is Daniel Hughes and my date of birth is the 14th of March 1988. Thank you, Mr. Hughes. I've verified your identity. I can see your account ending in 4471. Let me block that debit card right now so it cannot be used. Please hurry. And can you check the recent transactions? I have a horrible feeling someone has already taken money out. There were a couple of payments. I definitely did not make. Of course, the card is now blocked, so no further payments can go through. Let me review the latest activity with you. I can see two transactions this morning, 40 lbs at an electronics shop and Ł250 to an online retailer. This is unbelievable. I did not authorise either of those. Ł250. How could you let this happen? I want that money back immediately. I completely understand your frustration and I'm sorry this has happened. I'm raising a fraud dispute for both transactions right now. Our fraud team will investigate and in most cases the funds are refunded within 5 working days. Wait, I'm a bit confused. Does that mean I have to pay for them in the meantime? And how will I know if the refund has actually gone through? Good questions. No, you will not be charged while the dispute is open. The amounts will be temporarily credited back. You'll receive a text message and an e-mail at each stage, and a final confirmation once the investigation closes. Alright, that makes sense. Thank you for explaining it so clearly. You're welcome. Now, shall I order a replacement debit card for you? It will arrive within three to five working days at your registered address. Yes, please order the new card. Is the address still 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester? That's correct. I've ordered your replacement card to 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester. There's normally a Ł5 fee for an urgent replacement. A fee. Honestly after all this you're going to charge me Ł5 because someone stole my card? That doesn't feel fair at all. You make a fairpoint and given the circumstances, I'm going to waive that fee for you. There will be no charge whatsoever for the replacement. Thank you. I'm just. I'm really shaken by all this. I've never had anything like this happen before and it's left me feeling quite anxious about my money. That's a completely natural reaction and I want to reassure you that your account is now fully secured, the card is blocked, the fraudulent charges are under dispute and your new card is on its way. You're fully protected by our fraud guarantee. And just to be safe, should I be checking anything else? Like my online banking password or my other accounts? That's a sensible step. I'd recommend changing your online banking password as a precaution. I've also checked your savings account and there's no unusual activity there. It's all secure. That's a relief. While I have you, could you tell me my current account balance? Certainly, after the temporary credit for the disputed amounts, your available balance is 1320 lbs. Oh, that's much better than I feared. You've genuinely put my mind at ease, Sophie. I was in a real panic when I called. I'm so glad I could help. To summarise, your old card is blocked. Two transactions are under fraud investigation. A free replacement card is on the way and you'll get updates by text and e-mail. Is there anything else I can do for you today? No, that's everything. Thank you so much for your patience and for sorting all of this out so quickly. I really do appreciate it. It's been my pleasure, Mr. Hughes. Take care, stay safe and thank you for banking with Northbridge. Goodbye. '''
</transcript>

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an advanced call center summarization assistant. Your task is to generate a structured and concise summary of a conversation between a customer and an agent.

The input text will appear in Dialogue format (e.g., "Customer: …" / "Agent: …").

Your output MUST:
- Be strictly within a TOTAL limit of 200 words (count all headings and bullets).
- Contain ONLY the following THREE sections, in the same order, with the exact headings shown below.
- Use concise bullet points that start with "- " (dash + space). Each bullet must be 1–2 short sentences.

If there is no information available for a section, write:
- For 'Resolution Provided:' use exactly: No resolution provided
- For 'Follow-up Actions:' use exactly: No follow-up needed

Customer Query:
- …

Resolution Provided:
- …

Follow-up Actions:
- …

Important rules:
• Do not include or reproduce masked PII (e.g., [PERSON], [ORG], [CURRENCY], [ACCOUNT]) or any other personal/identifying details.
• Always refer to the parties only as “the customer” and “the agent.”
• Keep each bullet point short and factual.
• Do not provide verbatim dialogue. Focus only on the customer query, what resolution was provided, and follow-up (if any).
• Customer Query must capture ALL major concerns/issues raised by the customer (combine multiple issues under this section).
• Do not add any extra sections, labels, commentary, or text outside the required headings and bullets.

[INTENT] Debit Card_Lost or Stolen_Block
[SENTIMENT] Happy
[SUMMARY]
Customer Query:
- The customer reported a stolen debit card and suspected unauthorized transactions.
- Concerned about potential fraudulent charges and requested urgent action.
- Asked about the process for disputing charges and refund timelines.
- Inquired about replacement card delivery and associated fees.
- Expressed anxiety about account security and asked for advice on further precautions.
- Requested current account balance information.

Resolution Provided:
- The agent immediately blocked the stolen debit card to prevent further use.
- Verified two unauthorized transactions totaling Ł290 and initiated a fraud dispute.
- Assured the customer no charges would apply during the dispute and temporary credits would be applied.
- Confirmed the customer would receive updates via text and email throughout the investigation.
- Ordered a replacement debit card to the registered address and waived the usual Ł5 urgent replacement fee.
- Recommended changing the online banking password as a precaution.
- Verified no unusual activity on the customer’s savings account.
- Provided the updated available balance after temporary credits.

Follow-up Actions:
- Fraud team to investigate disputed transactions with expected resolution within five working days.
- Customer to monitor communications for updates on the dispute and replacement card delivery.

[DEBUG] System prompt:
You are an Intent Identification engine for call-center dialogs (voice or chat).

You will be given TWO inputs:

1) A conversation transcript: provided in the user message inside a <transcript> block.
2) An intent library (INTENT_LIBRARY): provided in a separate system message.


Your task:
Identify the best customer intent using the rules below and return ONLY a single JSON object in the exact output schema specified.

========================
OUTPUT SCHEMA (STRICT)
========================

Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations. No markdown. No extra text.

{
  "Intent": "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>",
  "KAIQuery": "<one short, crisp question derived from the transcript>",
  "Intent_source": "lastCustomerTurn" or "fullTranscript"
}

========================
FIELD DEFINITIONS
========================

Intent:
- Must ALWAYS follow the format Product_Service_Action.
- If matched from INTENT_LIBRARY, return the Intent value EXACTLY as defined in the library.
- If generated (no match), construct using inferred Product, Service, Action.
- If any field is missing, use "Unknown".

LIBRARY PRIORITY RULE (VERY IMPORTANT):
- The intent library is authoritative and curated by business experts.
- You MUST strongly prefer selecting the closest matching intent from INTENT_LIBRARY rather than generating a new intent.
- Even if the wording in the transcript is different from the library entry, choose the library intent if the meaning is substantially similar.
- Do NOT attempt to invent a "better", "cleaner", or more specific intent if a reasonably close library intent already exists.
- Only generate a new intent if NONE of the library entries reasonably match the customer's request.

ABSOLUTE FORMAT ENFORCEMENT (NON-NEGOTIABLE):
- The "Intent" value MUST contain EXACTLY 2 underscore characters ("_") and therefore EXACTLY 3 parts:
  Part1=Product, Part2=Service, Part3=Action.
- It must NEVER contain fewer or more than 3 parts.
- If you are about to output an Intent with only 1 or 2 parts (e.g., "Account_Block card" or "Block card_Request"),
  you MUST repair it BEFORE output:
    - If only 1 part is present: output "Unknown_Unknown_<that_part>" ONLY if that part is clearly an Action,
      otherwise "Unknown_<that_part>_Unknown" if it is clearly a Service,
      otherwise "<that_part>_Unknown_Unknown" if it is clearly a Product,
      otherwise "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".
    - If only 2 parts are present:
        * If it looks like Product_Service: append "_Unknown"
        * If it looks like Service_Action: prepend "Unknown_"
        * If it looks like Product_Action: insert "_Unknown_" in the middle
      If uncertain which pattern it is, default to: "<part1>_<part2>_Unknown".
- If the matched library Intent contains an invalid format (not exactly 3 parts), DO NOT output it as-is.
  Instead, repair it to exactly 3 parts using the same rules above.
- FINAL CHECK: Before returning JSON, count underscores in Intent:
  - If underscore count != 2, replace Intent with "Unknown_Unknown_Unknown".

KAIQuery:
- Must be ONE single line.
- Must be short and crisp.
- Must be a question.
- Prefer direct customer question from transcript.
- If not explicitly present, paraphrase the customer request into a short question.

Intent_source:
- "lastCustomerTurn" if identified primarily from the most recent customer turn in the transcript.
- "fullTranscript" if identified from earlier conversation context.

========================
INTENT IDENTIFICATION LOGIC (MANDATORY ORDER)
========================

STEP 1 — MOST RECENT REQUEST PRIORITY
- Examine the most recent customer turn in the transcript first.
- Compare its meaning against each item in INTENT_LIBRARY.
- Use the Product, Service and Action names for semantic matching.
- If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
    - Select that library Intent.
    - Intent_source = "lastCustomerTurn"
    - STOP.

STEP 2 — FULL TRANSCRIPT FALLBACK
- If no match in Step 1:
    - Examine the earlier conversation context in the transcript.
    - Attempt semantic match against INTENT_LIBRARY using the Product, Service and Action names.
    - If a clear or reasonably close match exists:
        - Return that library Intent.
        - Intent_source = "fullTranscript"
        - STOP.

STEP 3 — GENERATE NEW INTENT (ONLY IF NO REASONABLE LIBRARY MATCH EXISTS)
- This step should be used very rarely.
- Only proceed if ALL library intents clearly fail to represent the customer's request.
- Infer Product, Service, Action from the transcript meaning.
- You may use vocabulary hints from the INTENT_LIBRARY entries.
- If any field cannot be confidently inferred, set it to "Unknown".
- Construct Intent as:
    "<Product>_<Service>_<Action>"
- Intent_source should reflect where primary evidence was found 
  ("lastCustomerTurn" preferred if applicable).

========================
DISAMBIGUATION RULES
========================

- Always prioritize customer intent over agent statements.
- Prefer most recent actionable request.
- If multiple library intents are plausible, choose the single strongest semantic match.
- If a library intent captures most of the customer's request meaning, prefer that library intent instead of generating a new one.
- Do NOT return multiple intents.
- Do NOT invent new intents unless absolutely necessary.

========================
FINAL INSTRUCTION
========================

Return ONLY the JSON object.
No commentary.
No markdown.
No additional text.

''' Good afternoon. Thank you for calling Northbridge Bank. My name is Sophie. How may I help you today? Hello, Sophie. I I think my debit card has been stolen. I can't find it anywhere and I'm really worried someone might be using it right now. I'm very sorry to hear that. Please don't worry, we'll secure your account straight away for security. Could you please confirm your full name and date of birth? Yes, of course. My name is Daniel Hughes and my date of birth is the 14th of March 1988. Thank you, Mr. Hughes. I've verified your identity. I can see your account ending in 4471. Let me block that debit card right now so it cannot be used. Please hurry. And can you check the recent transactions? I have a horrible feeling someone has already taken money out. There were a couple of payments. I definitely did not make. Of course, the card is now blocked, so no further payments can go through. Let me review the latest activity with you. I can see two transactions this morning, 40 lbs at an electronics shop and Ł250 to an online retailer. This is unbelievable. I did not authorise either of those. Ł250. How could you let this happen? I want that money back immediately. I completely understand your frustration and I'm sorry this has happened. I'm raising a fraud dispute for both transactions right now. Our fraud team will investigate and in most cases the funds are refunded within 5 working days. Wait, I'm a bit confused. Does that mean I have to pay for them in the meantime? And how will I know if the refund has actually gone through? Good questions. No, you will not be charged while the dispute is open. The amounts will be temporarily credited back. You'll receive a text message and an e-mail at each stage, and a final confirmation once the investigation closes. Alright, that makes sense. Thank you for explaining it so clearly. You're welcome. Now, shall I order a replacement debit card for you? It will arrive within three to five working days at your registered address. Yes, please order the new card. Is the address still 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester? That's correct. I've ordered your replacement card to 12 Maple Gardens, Manchester. There's normally a Ł5 fee for an urgent replacement. A fee. Honestly after all this you're going to charge me Ł5 because someone stole my card? That doesn't feel fair at all. You make a fairpoint and given the circumstances, I'm going to waive that fee for you. There will be no charge whatsoever for the replacement. Thank you. I'm just. I'm really shaken by all this. I've never had anything like this happen before and it's left me feeling quite anxious about my money. That's a completely natural reaction and I want to reassure you that your account is now fully secured, the card is blocked, the fraudulent charges are under dispute and your new card is on its way. You're fully protected by our fraud guarantee. And just to be safe, should I be checking anything else? Like my online banking password or my other accounts? That's a sensible step. I'd recommend changing your online banking password as a precaution. I've also checked your savings account and there's no unusual activity there. It's all secure. That's a relief. While I have you, could you tell me my current account balance? Certainly, after the temporary credit for the disputed amounts, your available balance is 1320 lbs. Oh, that's much better than I feared. You've genuinely put my mind at ease, Sophie. I was in a real panic when I called. I'm so glad I could help. To summarise, your old card is blocked. Two transactions are under fraud investigation. A free replacement card is on the way and you'll get updates by text and e-mail. Is there anything else I can do for you today? No, that's everything. Thank you so much for your patience and for sorting all of this out so quickly. I really do appreciate it. It's been my pleasure, Mr. Hughes. Take care, stay safe and thank you for banking with Northbridge. Goodbye. Goodbye Sophie, and thanks again. '''
</transcript>

You are an advanced sentiment classification assistant for call center conversations. Your task is to analyze the given conversation and determine the customer’s sentiment, based on the last sentence the customer spoke. The conversation may appear either in a dialogue format (with markers such as “Customer:” and “Agent:”) or as a mixed paragraph of text.

Rules (Unbreakable):

•	Always identify the final customer turn(the last one or two sentences spoken by the customer). 
•   If the last line is from the agent, look back to the most recent customer turn.
•   Use the overall tone and intent expressed in these final sentences by the customer to determine sentiment.
•	Your answer must be exactly one word, and that word must be one of the following six options only:


[Happy, Neutral, Confusion, Fear, Sad, Anger]

•	You are not allowed to output anything else.
•	If the conversation is ambiguous or incomplete, you must still select the closest possible option from the six above.
•	Any output other than one of these six exact words is invalid and will be rejected.
•	Output must be only the sentiment word, without labels, punctuation, or quotes.

Input: Thanks a lot, you’ve been great, wonderful service.
Happy

Input: I need a statement, check balance, how do I…

Input: I don’t understand, can you explain again?, what does it mean?
Confusion

Fear

Sad

Anger

Happy

- Be strictly within a TOTAL limit of 200 words (count all headings and bullets).
- Contain ONLY the following THREE sections, in the same order, with the exact headings shown below.
- Use concise bullet points that start with "- " (dash + space). Each bullet must be 1–2 short sentences.

- For 'Resolution Provided:' use exactly: No resolution provided
- For 'Follow-up Actions:' use exactly: No follow-up needed

Customer Query:
- …

Resolution Provided:
- …

Follow-up Actions:
- …

• Do not include or reproduce masked PII (e.g., [PERSON], [ORG], [CURRENCY], [ACCOUNT]) or any other personal/identifying details.

• Keep each bullet point short and factual.

[INTENT] Debit Card_Lost or Stolen_Block
[SENTIMENT] Happy
[SUMMARY]
Customer Query:
- The customer reported a stolen debit card and suspected unauthorized transactions.
- He was worried about potential fraudulent use and wanted the card blocked immediately.
- The customer requested a review of recent transactions and a refund for unauthorized charges.
- He inquired about the payment responsibility during the dispute and how to track the refund.
- The customer asked for a replacement card and questioned the replacement fee.
- He expressed anxiety about account security and sought advice on additional precautions.
- The customer also requested his current account balance.

Resolution Provided:
- The agent blocked the stolen debit card to prevent further use.
- Two unauthorized transactions were identified and a fraud dispute was initiated.
- The customer was assured no charges would apply during the dispute and temporary credits would be applied.
- Updates on the investigation would be sent via text and email.
- A replacement debit card was ordered to the registered address with the replacement fee waived.
- The agent recommended changing the online banking password as a precaution.
- The agent confirmed no unusual activity on the savings account.
- The current account balance after temporary credits was provided.

Follow-up Actions:
- The fraud team will investigate the disputed transactions and process refunds within five working days.
- The customer will receive ongoing updates about the dispute status.
- No further follow-up needed from the customer at this time.
